anyformat Release 0.0.2
Release 0.0.2  0.0.2
The official Python library for the anyformat API
Homepage PyPI Python
License
:   Apache-2.0
Install
:   pip install anyformat==0.0.2
Documentation
Anyformat Python API library
The Anyformat Python library provides convenient access to the Anyformat REST API from any Python 3.9+ application. The library includes type definitions for all request params and response fields, and offers both synchronous and asynchronous clients powered by httpx.
It is generated with Stainless.
The full API of this library can be found in api.md.
Installation
# install from PyPI #
Usage
The full API of this library can be found in api.md.
import os from anyformat import Anyformat client = Anyformat api_key = os environ get "ANYFORMAT_API_KEY" # This is the default and can be omitted response = client health check
While you can provide an api_key keyword argument, we recommend using python-dotenv to add ANYFORMAT_API_KEY="My API Key" to your .env file so that your API Key is not stored in source control.
Async usage
Simply import AsyncAnyformat instead of Anyformat and use await with each API call:
import os import asyncio from anyformat import AsyncAnyformat client = AsyncAnyformat api_key = os environ get "ANYFORMAT_API_KEY" # This is the default and can be omitted async def main -> None response = await client health check asyncio run main
Functionality between the synchronous and asynchronous clients is otherwise identical.
With aiohttp
By default, the async client uses httpx for HTTP requests. However, for improved concurrency performance you may also use aiohttp as the HTTP backend.
You can enable this by installing aiohttp:
# install from PyPI #
Then you can enable it by instantiating the client with http_client=DefaultAioHttpClient():
import os import asyncio from anyformat import DefaultAioHttpClient from anyformat import AsyncAnyformat async def main -> None async with AsyncAnyformat api_key = os environ get "ANYFORMAT_API_KEY" # This is the default and can be omitted http_client = DefaultAioHttpClient as client response = await client health check asyncio run main
Using types
Nested request parameters are TypedDicts. Responses are Pydantic models which also provide helper methods for things like:
 Serializing back into JSON, model.to_json()
 Converting to a dictionary, model.to_dict()
Typed requests and responses provide autocomplete and documentation within your editor. If you would like to see type errors in VS Code to help catch bugs earlier, set python.analysis.typeCheckingMode to basic.
Handling errors
When the library is unable to connect to the API (for example, due to network connection problems or a timeout), a subclass of anyformat.APIConnectionError is raised.
When the API returns a non-success status code (that is, 4xx or 5xx response), a subclass of anyformat.APIStatusError is raised, containing status_code and response properties.
All errors inherit from anyformat.APIError.
import anyformat from anyformat import Anyformat client = Anyformat try client health check except anyformat APIConnectionError as e print "The server could not be reached" print e __cause__# an underlying Exception, likely raised within httpx. except anyformat RateLimitError as e print"A 429 status code was received; we should back off a bit." except anyformat APIStatusError as e print"Another non-200-range status code was received" print e status_code print e response
Error codes are as follows:
| Status Code | Error Type |
 --- |
| 400 | BadRequestError |
| 401 | AuthenticationError |
| 403 | PermissionDeniedError |
| 404 | NotFoundError |
| 422 | UnprocessableEntityError |
| 429 | RateLimitError |
| >=500 | InternalServerError |
| N/A | APIConnectionError |
Retries
Certain errors are automatically retried 2 times by default, with a short exponential backoff. Connection errors (for example, due to a network connectivity problem), 408 Request Timeout, 409 Conflict, 429 Rate Limit, and >=500 Internal errors are all retried by default.
You can use the max_retries option to configure or disable retry settings:
from anyformat import Anyformat# Configure the default for all requests: client = Anyformat # default is 2 max_retries = 0# Or, configure per-request: client with_options max_retries = 5 health check
Timeouts
By default requests time out after 1 minute. You can configure this with a timeout option, which accepts a float or an httpx.Timeout object:
from anyformat import Anyformat# Configure the default for all requests: client = Anyformat# 20 seconds (default is 1 minute) timeout =20.0# More granular control: client = Anyformat timeout = httpx Timeout60.0 read =5.0 write =10.0 connect =2.0# Override per-request: client with_options timeout =5.0 health check
On timeout, an APITimeoutError is thrown.
Note that requests that time out are retried twice by default.
Advanced
Logging
We use the standard library logging module.
You can enable logging by setting the environment variable ANYFORMAT_LOG to info.
export
Or to debug for more verbose logging.
How to tell whether None means null or missing
In an API response, a field may be explicitly null, or missing entirely; in either case, its value is None in this library. You can differentiate the two cases with .model_fields_set:
if response my_field is None if 'my_field' not in not in response model_fields_set print'Got json like {}, without a "my_field" key present at all.' else print'Got json like {"my_field": null}.'
Accessing raw response data (e.g. headers)
The "raw" Response object can be accessed by prefixing .with_raw_response. to any HTTP method call, e.g.,
from anyformat import Anyformat client = Anyformat response = client health with_raw_response check print response headers get'X-My-Header' health = response parse# get the object that 
These methods return an [The async client returns an AsyncAPIResponse with the same structure, the only difference being awaitable methods for reading the response content.
.with_streaming_response
The above interface eagerly reads the full response body when you make the request, which may not always be what you want.
To stream the response body, use .with_streaming_response instead, which requires a context manager and only reads the response body once you call .read(), .text(), .json(), .iter_bytes(), .iter_text(), .iter_lines() or .parse(). In the async client, these are async methods.
with client health with_streaming_response check as response print response headers get"X-My-Header" for line in response iter_lines print line
The context manager is required so that the response will reliably be closed.
Making custom/undocumented requests
This library is typed for convenient access to the documented API.
If you need to access undocumented endpoints, params, or response properties, the library can still be used.
Undocumented endpoints
To make requests to undocumented endpoints, you can make requests using client.get, client.post, and other http verbs. Options on the client will be respected (such as retries) when making this request.
import httpx response = client post"/foo" cast_to = httpx Response body = "my_param" True print response headers get"x-foo"
Undocumented request params
If you want to explicitly send an extra param, you can do so with the extra_query, extra_body, and extra_headers request options.
Undocumented response properties
To access undocumented response properties, you can access the extra fields like response.unknown_prop. You can also get all the extra fields on the Pydantic model as a dict with response.model_extra.
Configuring the HTTP client
You can directly override the httpx client to customize it for your use case, including:
 Support for proxies
 Custom transports
 Additional advanced functionality
import httpx from anyformat import Anyformat DefaultHttpxClient client = Anyformat # Or use the 
You can also customize the client on a per-request basis by using client with_options http_client = DefaultHttpxClient
Managing HTTP resources
By default the library closes underlying HTTP connections whenever the client is garbage collected. You can manually close the client using the .close() method if desired, or with a context manager that closes when exiting.
from anyformat import Anyformat with Anyformat as client # make requests here # HTTP client is now closed
Versioning
This package generally follows SemVer conventions, though certain backwards-incompatible changes may be released as minor versions:
1. Changes that only affect static types, without breaking runtime behavior.
2. Changes to library internals which are technically public but not intended or documented for external use. (Please open a GitHub issue to let us know if you are relying on such internals.)
3. Changes that we do not expect to impact the vast majority of users in practice.
We take backwards-compatibility seriously and work hard to ensure you can rely on a smooth upgrade experience.
We are keen for your feedback; please open an issue with questions, bugs, or suggestions.
Determining the installed version
If you've upgraded to the latest version but aren't seeing any new features you were expecting then your python environment is likely still using an older version.
You can determine the version that is being used at runtime with:
import anyformat print anyformat __version__
Python 3.9 or higher.
See the contributing documentation.

Anyformat - overview
Established
2024
Location
Madrid, -, Spain
Primary Industry
Software
About
Established in 2024 and based in Madrid, Spain, Anyformat focuses on document processing technologies. Alejandro Fernandez Rodriguez (COO), Juan Huguet Garcia (CEO), and Diego Perez Sastre (CTO) are the co-founders of the company. In November 2025, Anyformat raised EUR 3. 3 million in seed funding led by new investor Kibo Ventures, with participation from another new investor Decelera Ventures.
Returning investors 4Founders Capital and Abac Capital also participated in the round.
Anyformat specializes in advanced data extraction and structuring solutions, enabling organizations to derive insights from various document formats, including emails, PDFs, scans, reports, and audio recordings. The company’s core offering utilizes proprietary in-house models optimized for specific use cases, allowing for automated extraction of complex and heterogeneous documents without the risk of hallucination. This technology is particularly beneficial for industries requiring the processing of lengthy documents, such as financial services, healthcare, and insurance.
Anyformat serves a diverse client base, including leading companies across multiple sectors, who leverage the service to streamline processes such as data reconciliation, regulatory compliance, and customer relationship management. The products are deployed in private cloud or on-premises environments, ensuring data security and privacy while being utilized in global markets.
The revenue model of Anyformat is centered around B2B transactions, where enterprises engage the company’s services through subscription-based agreements or tailored partnerships.
The company plans to utilize the November 2025 funding to grow its engineering and product teams, which will help advance its roadmap for enhanced extraction features and the creation of unique error detection models.

supporting_evidence.title: Why We Invested in AnyFormat – Turning Data Chaos into ...
supporting_evidence.snippet:
Why We Invested in AnyFormat
At Decelera, we look for teams that combine technical mastery with strategic clarity. Juan, Diego, and Alejandro have worked at the frontier of AI and data infrastructure, most notably at Clarity AI, one of the fastest-scaling impact-tech companies in Europe. They know what it takes to build robust, enterprise-ready AI products.
But beyond their credentials, they have built a platform that solves a real pain point for modern businesses, and does so with precision, speed, and empathy for the end user.
Juan: The Scientist Who Sees the System [...] Alejandro: The Strategic Operator
Alejandro Fernández brings structure and momentum. With experience across EY, PwC, and Clarity AI, he drives operations with the precision of an engineer and the insight of a strategist. At AnyFormat, he makes sure the solution doesn’t just work, but fits perfectly into clients’ most critical workflows. [...] Juan Huguet, CEO & Co-founder of Anyformat at #DeceleraMenorca2025
The Challenge: Unstructured Data Is Everywhere
Modern organizations spend millions trying to extract and standardize data buried in scattered formats. Legacy systems break down, manual processes are slow and error-prone, and scalability remains a dream.
AnyFormat changes the game. With seamless integrations, enterprise-grade security, and rapid workflow configuration, it enables teams to extract, validate, and export structured data at scale, without writing a single line of code.
Why We Invested in AnyFormat
supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Saltar al contenido 
Decelera
Contact
Decelera
Contact
Why We Invested in AnyFormat – Turning Data Chaos into Clarity
Escrito por Marina Bestué
At Decelera Ventures, we back founders who tackle deep, structural inefficiencies with bold vision and elegant execution. AnyFormat does exactly that, turning the overwhelming chaos of unstructured data into clean, actionable insight.
From insurance to banking, companies today are drowning in PDFs, images, emails, and audio files filled with valuable information, but trapped in formats machines can’t understand. AnyFormat unlocks that data with a GenAI platform that delivers over 99% accuracy, reduces costs by more than 90%, and requires zero technical expertise to implement.

Juan Huguet, CEO & Co-founder of Anyformat at #DeceleraMenorca2025
The Challenge: Unstructured Data Is Everywhere
Modern organizations spend millions trying to extract and standardize data buried in scattered formats. Legacy systems break down, manual processes are slow and error-prone, and scalability remains a dream.
AnyFormat changes the game. With seamless integrations, enterprise-grade security, and rapid workflow configuration, it enables teams to extract, validate, and export structured data at scale, without writing a single line of code.
Why We Invested in AnyFormat
At Decelera, we look for teams that combine technical mastery with strategic clarity. Juan, Diego, and Alejandro have worked at the frontier of AI and data infrastructure, most notably at Clarity AI, one of the fastest-scaling impact-tech companies in Europe. They know what it takes to build robust, enterprise-ready AI products.
But beyond their credentials, they have built a platform that solves a real pain point for modern businesses, and does so with precision, speed, and empathy for the end user.
Juan: The Scientist Who Sees the System
With a PhD in Physics and a deep background in machine learning, Juan Huguet doesn’t just lead, he architects intelligence. At IBM and Clarity AI, he led complex AI projects; now, as CEO of AnyFormat, he channels that experience into creating infrastructure that scales effortlessly while adapting to real-world use cases.
Diego: The Quiet Power Behind the Platform
As CTO, Diego Pérez builds what others call impossible. With a strong foundation in AI and MLOps, he ensures that AnyFormat’s core engine is both technically elegant and deeply reliable. Whether it’s designing robust model pipelines or architecting seamless scalability, Diego makes sure the engine runs with zero compromise.
Alejandro: The Strategic Operator
Alejandro Fernández brings structure and momentum. With experience across EY, PwC, and Clarity AI, he drives operations with the precision of an engineer and the insight of a strategist. At AnyFormat, he makes sure the solution doesn’t just work, but fits perfectly into clients’ most critical workflows.

Diego Pérez, Juan Huguet & Alejandro Fernández, founders of Anyformat 2024
Building the Future of Data
We believe AnyFormat is building foundational infrastructure for the GenAI era. In a world where data complexity grows exponentially, they are offering clarity, speed, and control. They aren’t just transforming how businesses extract data, they’re shaping how organizations make decisions.
At Decelera, we support founders who solve hard problems with lasting impact. That’s exactly what AnyFormat is doing. And we’re proud to back them.If you’re a founder looking to scale with purpose, apply for our next program and be part of a community that helps you grow beyond the grind.

Getting Started
Welcome to anyformat
Learn how to turn documents into reliable, structured data, without building fragile pipelines.
anyformat helps you extract, validate, and transform data from any document using AI-powered workflows. This documentation will guide you step by step, from your first file to advanced workflows and automation. Whether you’re here to:

​ Get started quickly

Files & Documents

API Quickstart
For developers and technical teams.Learn how to integrate anyformat programmatically.
​ What is anyformat?
anyformat is a document processing platform that lets you:
 Upload documents (PDFs, images, scans)
 Extract structured data using AI
 Validate and refine results
 Output clean data you can actually use
You don’t need to be technical. You don’t need to write code. You do need documents and a problem to solve. 
​ What can I do with anyformat?
With anyformat, you can:
 Extract data from invoices, receipts, IDs, contracts, and more
 Handle unstructured or messy documents
 Standardize data across different formats
 Build repeatable workflows for document processing
 Validate and improve quality over time
 Export structured data to your systems
All without managing OCR engines, brittle rules, or manual review processes. 
​ How anyformat works
At a simple level, anyformat works like this:
1
Upload a file
anyformat reads the document and understands its structure.
2
Define what you want
You describe the data you need using fields and instructions.
3
Refine & validate
Review results, fix mistakes, and improve accuracy.
4
Run at scale
Apply the same logic to thousands of documents.
5
Use the output
Export clean, structured data in formats your tools understand.
You can keep things simple, or go deeper as your needs grow. 
​ Key concepts you’ll learn here
Before diving in, it helps to understand a few core ideas:
 Files - The documents you upload
 Schemas - The structure of the data you want
 Fields - Individual pieces of information (date, amount, name…)
 Workflows - Repeatable processes to extract and validate data
 Outputs - The structured results you use elsewhere
Don’t worry, we’ll explain each one step by step.

 Already using the product? Jump directly to Workflows.

Logo
Logo
Logo

Logo
Madrid-based Anyformat raises €520k to simplify the automation of unstructured data extraction
Stefano De Marzo

Anyformat, a Spanish startup betting on a new era of collaboration between people and artificial intelligence, has announced the raising of €520k in a pre-seed financing round. This round, led by 4Founders Capital, Abac Nest Ventures, and prominent business angels, will allow anyformat to accelerate the development of its generative artificial intelligence platform, designed to transform the way companies manage and analyze unstructured data.
With this injection of capital, anyformat is consolidating itself as a key solution to automate the extraction and analysis of complex data, enabling any user – without the need for technical knowledge – to convert large volumes of unstructured information into strategic insights. This marks a crucial step for organizations looking to optimize processes and improve data-driven decision making.
According to the Data Cube report “Spain Artificial Intelligence Market Growth and Performance”, the artificial intelligence market in Spain is expected to reach $26.56 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 27.4%. This growth environment, combined with the exponential increase in the generation of unstructured data, reinforces the strategic relevance of anyformat for companies seeking operational agility and efficiency.
Juan Huguet, CEO of anyformat, said: “Our mission is clear: to enable organizations to adopt hybrid intelligence by allowing AI to do the heavy lifting of extracting and structuring data, and getting people to focus on solving strategic and creative problems. With anyformat, companies access valuable information quickly and without complications.”
The anyformat platform automates the extraction of information contained in documents, presentations or even voice recordings, simplifying what previously required a lot of manual work or advanced technical knowledge. In the words of Alejandro Fernández, COO of anyformat, “Many companies are held back by the difficulty of managing unstructured data. With our solution, we make that information immediately available to teams, ready to be integrated into tools such as Excel or business intelligence platforms.”
In addition to its focus on usability, anyformat is designed to scale easily, ensuring simple integration with existing systems. Diego Pérez, CTO of anyformat, added: “Our priority is to optimize workflows without compromising data security or privacy. We know that trust is key, and we ensure that information is handled responsibly and efficiently, allowing organizations to grow sustainably.”
Investors recognize anyformat’s disruptive potential. Joan Pina, partner at Abac Nest Ventures, commented: “At Abac Nest Ventures we have bet on anyformat because we believe in the vision of its founders and in the impact that its technology can have on the industry. Turning unstructured data into actionable knowledge is a growing need, and companies using anyformat are gaining competitiveness by quickly and efficiently integrating AI into their processes.”
With the new funding, anyformat is preparing to expand its team and operations, with an eye toward consolidating its presence in Spain and exploring opportunities in international markets. “At anyformat, we are committed to transforming unstructured information into a competitive advantage, allowing companies to not only access valuable information, but to use it intelligently to make strategic decisions that drive their growth and success,” concluded Juan Huguet.
Stefano De Marzo

Anyformat Secures €3.3M to Scale Document Intelligence

By Ekaterina Pisareva
November 27, 2025
Anyformat team
Credits: Anyformat official website
Spanish AI startup Anyformat has closed a €3.3 million seed round to accelerate the development of its document intelligence platform. The funding was led by Kibo Ventures, with continued support from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which participated in the company's previous round.
The Madrid-based company has built a generative AI platform that extracts and structures complex data from virtually any document type. Its technology enables large organizations to process contracts, invoices, emails, and presentations with what the company describes as human-level accuracy at machine speed, while keeping sensitive data under full organizational control.
A New Approach to Document Intelligence
What sets Anyformat apart is its ability to combine extracted document information with a company's existing proprietary data in real time. This means businesses can automate the analysis of unstructured documents while simultaneously cross-referencing them against internal records like customer databases, policies, product catalogs, or historical information.
For organizations juggling complex documentation across fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validation steps, reduces errors, and speeds up business-critical processes. The opportunity is significant: according to Dell Technologies' Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to turn their data into valuable, real-time insights.
"The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that enables companies to automate critical processes while preserving data sovereignty, an essential requirement in Europe. The founders' vision and talent, combined with the vast potential of AI in a large and growing market, were key factors in our decision to invest," says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner at Kibo Ventures.
Expanding Capabilities and European Reach
The fresh capital will fuel product development and grow the company's engineering and product teams. Priorities include strengthening advanced extraction capabilities and building proprietary error-detection models. Anyformat also plans to establish a dedicated sales team focused on Spain and the broader European Union market.
The company will continue investing in its privacy-first infrastructure and is working toward ISO 27001 certification, a requirement for many large corporations and public institutions.
Anyformat forecasts surpassing €1 million in annual recurring revenue in 2026, driven by European expansion and growing demand for automation, compliance tools, and advanced document processing solutions.
From Startup to Enterprise Partner
Founded by CEO Juan Huguet and CTO Diego Pérez, Anyformat has already secured projects with the Singapore Government, L'Oréal Group, and IAG Group. These partnerships demonstrate the platform's ability to operate in complex, high-demand environments.
"Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to help companies analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the level of security required by modern regulations," says Huguet. Pérez adds: "We are building technology that goes far beyond simple document digitization."
Since raising €520,000 in June 2025, the company has significantly expanded its capabilities beyond traditional document extraction. It now positions itself as the only provider capable of real-time integration between extracted document data and enterprise systems.
Investors Back Long-Term Vision
The seed round brings together a strong syndicate of Spanish and European investors. Kibo Ventures led the round, while 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures all doubled down on their earlier investments. The continued backing from all previous investors signals confidence in Anyformat's trajectory and market positioning.
The company continues refining its technology to convert any file into structured data for analytics, business intelligence, and operational workflows, always maintaining strict privacy and security standards.
More about:  AI | Europe | Funding | Generative AI | Seed Funding | Spain | Startups | Venture capital

HomeFundingWith €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand out across AI platforms
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
October 16, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower).
“_AI assistants are becoming the front door to the internet_,” says Daniel Espejo, CEO and Co-founder of Omnia. “_Omnia gives teams the playbook and the agents to act on it. Moving from dashboards to done. Just like Cursor makes engineers 10× more productive, Omnia makes marketers 10× more effective on the channels that matter now_.”
Omnia’s pre-Seed round aligns with a growing wave of European investment into agentic and AI-driven marketing platforms in 2025.
Comparable early-stage rounds include Massive Dynamic (€3 million, Paris) and Tandem (€3.2 million, Paris), both developing AI-based orchestration tools. Further up the scale, Sweden’s EvoluteIQ secured €44 million to expand its enterprise agentic-AI suite, while Italy’s SylloTips (€4.2 million) and the Netherlands’ Aizy (€1.5 million) also closed funding to advance AI-based marketing and agent platforms.
Within this context, there haven’t been many comparable funding rounds in Spain, positioning Omnia as an early mover in Spain’s emerging agentic-AI marketing ecosystem.
“_As search shifts rapidly from browsers to AI engines and assistants, every brand will need an agentic strategy to stay visible,_” saidRobert Jaeckle, Partner at Visionaries Club. “_Omnia is pioneering this space by building the agentic platform that turns visibility into action for marketeers. We’re so excited to partner with Daniel and Miguel as they define this new category for Europe and beyond._”
Omnia was founded in 2025 by Daniel Espejo (CEO), formerly at Klarna where he launched markets and scaled products used by millions, and Miguel Fernández (CTO), a platform engineer with experience at GitHub and Fastly.
Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.
Since launch, Omnia has been adopted by brands including Personio, ElevenLabs, Embat, Línea Directa, Ironhack, Air Europa, Praktika.ai , Trade Republic, Pleo and HappyRobot. ​
Also by marketing agencies like MIO group, Elogia, Product Hackers and Everybody. Early customers are allegedly reporting up to 3× visibility gains across AI channels.
“_Omnia has been key to our growth – helping us understand what potential clients are searching for, how well we’re positioned, and how to capture leads from AI across different markets,_” shared Adrian Varez, Head of Growth at Happy Robot.
The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.

HomeFundingMadrid-based startup Shakers raises €14 million to help businesses integrate teams of...
FundingSpain-Startups
Madrid-based startup Shakers raises €14 million to help businesses integrate teams of specialised talent with AI
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
HRTech startup Shakers, a Spanish workforce management platform connecting companies with tech freelancers, today announced its €14 million Series A funding round in order to expand double its headcount.
The funding round was led by Partech, with further investment from KFund, Brighteye Ventures, Athos Capital and Wayra.
Héctor Mata, Shakers CEO, said: “The convergence of AI and independent talent is not a trend – it’s a new operating model. Demand for specialised technical talent is soaring, but the traditional outsourcing model hasn’t evolved in over decades. With Shakers, we are setting a new standard for hiring: our platform acts as the new operating system for performance-driven freelancers — giving them the same benefits as traditional roles, while offering companies the agility to innovate faster. As we expand across Europe, we’re building the infrastructure layer that powers the AI-infused workplace of the future.”
Founded in 2021, Shakers is a workforce orchestration platform that connects businesses with specialised tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents. Founded by four serial entrepreneurs – Héctor Mata (CEO), Nico de Luis (COO), Adrián de Pedro (CPO) and Jaime Castillo (CRO) – with backgrounds in AI and software development, and first-hand experience in both finding freelance work and hiring talent.
With over 10,000 vetted freelancers and 450+ client companies including Microsoft, Inditex and Telefónica, Shakers aims to empower a new generation of performance-driven freeworkers with integrated services such as banking, insurance and productivity tools – all within its all-in-one Freelancer OS.
The company has grown over 6x in revenue over the past year, and is aiming to continue on its growth trajectory. 60% of their revenue reportedly comes from recurring customers and 30% of revenue generated organically from other European markets.
Shakers envisions a future of work where businesses can easily onboard specialised teams of tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents to collaborate, experiment, and drive new technological capabilities via complex and long-term projects. The platform empowers businesses and freelancers to automate their most “frustrating” workflow challenges and upgrade their productivity skills, promoting quick and effective results.
According to data provided by Shakers, this comes at a time when the traditional IT outsourcing landscape is being reshaped, with the European market projected to grow from €180 billion to over €280 billion by 2030 as demand shifts toward more flexible, high-skill talent solutions.
Shakers plans to double its team size in the next year to facilitate their growth, while expanding its AI agent integrations for freelancers and businesses. By 2027, Shakers will reportedly have orchestrated over 10,000 hybrid projects.
Simone Riva, Partech Senior Principal, said: “At Partech, we believe that while AI is reshaping the way we work, exceptional talent will always be at the heart of the future of work. From our very first conversation, it was clear the Shakers team is building something special — a fast, intuitive platform tailored to the flexible workforce of tomorrow. They’re redefining how companies find, hire, and collaborate with top talent. We’re proud to back this visionary team and are excited to support them as they scale across Spain, Europe, and beyond.”
As part of Shakers’ community of 10,000+ tech freelancers, they will enjoy the same benefits as traditional employment, including embedded services for banking, insurance, productivity and more, alongside the flexibility, freedom and incentives of self-employment.
According to Shakers, their technology goes beyond just sourcing – it validates talent end-to-end, assessing both hard and soft skills, experience, and cultural fit through more than 1,000 data points.
Integrated with Shakers Verified, their pre-vetting tool, the platform eliminates the need for early interviews and technical tests, delivering ready-to-work talent from day one. This means that companies can potentially go from idea to fully assembled team in minutes.

Executive Summary
Value Proposition & Problem: Mews offers a cloud‑native property management system that eliminates manual, fragmented hotel operations, enabling hoteliers to focus on guest experience and profitability.
Product / Service: A unified hospitality platform covering PMS, payments, revenue management, bookings, and marketplace integrations, with AI‑driven automation and an open API.
Market Stage & Traction: Over 12,500 properties in 85 countries use Mews, representing a customer base growth of 85% year‑over‑year and a transaction volume of $19.7 billion in 2025.
Achievements: Recognized as Best PMS (2024‑2026) and Best POS (2026) by Hotel Tech Report, 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% rise in RevPAR, and 70% average RevPAG uplift for customers.

Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World's Leading Hospitality Operating System
Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Published January 22, 2026 Press EQT Growth leads Series D funding round to accelerate the AI-native future of hotels globally, with participation from new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Investment values Mews at $2.5 billion Mews’ Platform Transaction Volume reached $19.7 billion in 2025 Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: [New York, 22 January 2026] – Mews , the operating system for hospitality, today announced it has raised $300 million in a Series D funding round led by EQT Growth , with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners , and participation from existing investors Kinnevik , Battery Ventures and Tiger Global . This investment marks a defining moment for hospitality technology and values the company at $2.5 billion. Following a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%, this fundraise establishes Mews as the leading hospitality tech provider for hotels of all sizes. In 2025, the company achieved: Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: - A customer base of 15,000, with 132,000+ monthly active hoteliers in 85 countries - 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk 1 - Platform Transaction Volumes of $19.7 billion - Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% - Created more than half a billion ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces 2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations “With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry,” said Matt Welle, CEO of Mews. “We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in artificial intelligence, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed - enabling properties to: - Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation - Optimize revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior - Scale business without the debt of legacy tech - Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “Hospitality is the business of experiences,” said Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry.” A new standard for a global industry Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale,” said Kirk Lepke, Partner at EQT Growth. “Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Mews will also accelerate the expansion of Mews Payments and its broader fintech infrastructure, embedding commerce into the core of hotel operations and positioning Mews as the connector between experience and revenue. In addition, the company will continue its ambitious international expansion across its primary geographies of North America and Europe, as well as expanding into new markets. ... Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: The investment comes shortly after the completion of Mews’ fourteenth acquisition, DataChat, the leading generative AI analytics platform. In January 2026, Mews was named the #1 Property Management System for the third year in a row and #1 Hotel Point of Sale System in the world by customers and reviewers of Hotel Tech Report, a reflection of its ability to lead both the operational and commercial layers of hospitality. The funding round is subject to customary regulatory closing conditions. 1 A self‑service check‑in and check‑out kiosk that allows guests to handle arrival and departure themselves. 2 Mews’ multi‑space management capability that lets a property manage and sell any kind of space (not just rooms) directly in the property management system (PMS). About Mews Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Mews is the operating system for hospitality, unifying workflows across revenue, operations and the guest journey so teams can automate the mundane and focus on memorable guest experiences. The Mews platform spans PMS, POS, RMS, Housekeeping and Payments, helping hoteliers move from property management to profit management. Funding Netherland-Startups Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content:  By David Cendon Garcia January 22, 2026 Share Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Facebook Twitter WhatsApp Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Linkedin Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content:  Amsterdam-based Mews , an OS for hospitality, today announced it has raised €255 million ($300 million) in a Series D funding round in order to further invest in AI, embed agent-driven systems to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, improve the guest experience, and accelerate how products are built and deployed The round was led by EQT Growth, with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners, and participation from existing investors Kinnevik, Battery Ventures and Tiger Global – valuing the company at €2.1 billion ($2.5 billion). Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry, ” says Matt Welle , CEO of Mews. “ We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling .” In the context of recent European hospitality and HotelTech funding activity, Amsterdam-based Mews’ €255 million Series D represents one of the largest pure-play software raises in the sector. ... Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Taken together, these announcements indicate that roughly €695 million has been committed to European hospitality and adjacent HotelTech models in 2025/2026, with Mews’ raise accounting for a substantial share of that total. Also of note, is that in March 2024 Mews also raised €101 million to help hoteliers embrace cloud technology . “ Hospitality is the business of experiences, ” adds Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “ The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry. ” Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Founded in 2012, Mews an OS for hospitality, unifying workflows across revenue, operations and the guest journey so teams can automate the mundane and focus on memorable guest experiences. Powering 15,000 customers across 85 countries, the Mews platform spans PMS, POS, RMS, Housekeeping and Payments, helping hoteliers move from property management to profit management. This new funding follows a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%. In 2025, the company achieved: 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk1 Platform Transaction Volumes of €16.7 billion ($19.7 billion) Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% Created more than €457 million ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in AI, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed – enabling properties to: Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation Optimise revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior Scale business without the debt of legacy tech Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale ,” said Kirk Lepke , Partner at EQT Growth. “ Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews .” Mews will also accelerate the expansion of Mews Payments and its broader FinTech infrastructure, embedding commerce into the core of hotel operations and positioning Mews as the connector between experience and revenue. The investment comes shortly after the completion of Mews’ fourteenth acquisition, DataChat, a generative AI analytics platform. Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ Matt and Richard have built a category-defining platform with the depth, pace of innovation and global reach required by modern hospitality ,” said Laura Connell , Partner at Atomico. “ The most ambitious builders in hospitality are focused on delivering ever-improving experiences for their end consumers, and they need technology that can keep pace with rising expectations around speed, service and personalisation. Mews is the key enabler for the future of hospitality, and the team is well on their way to building a generational company .” Share Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Facebook Twitter WhatsApp Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Linkedin Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... What are the use cases of Mews?Copy Link
1. Use Cases by Category
   Front‑Desk Automation: Streamlines check‑in, check‑out and housekeeping communication. 
   Self‑service kiosks reduce check‑in time by 5 minutes.
   Digital key issuance eliminates front‑desk bottlenecks.
   Automated housekeeping notifications cut call volume.
   Revenue Management & Upselling: Optimizes pricing and drives ancillary sales. 
   Dynamic pricing engine (Atomize) boosts RevPAR by 39%.
   Embedded upsell offers increase average RevPAG by 70%.
   Direct‑booking engine lifts bookings by 45%.
   Guest Experience Personalisation: Creates seamless, end‑to‑end journeys. 
   Mews Guest Journey platform unifies booking, check‑in, in‑stay services.
   Online check‑in/out and mobile payments cut guest wait times.
   Smart‑device integrations (e.g., Operto) enable contact‑free stays.
   Multi‑Property & Chain Management: Centralises operations across dozens of locations. 
   Single dashboard controls 12,500+ properties worldwide.
   Unified reporting provides real‑time KPI visibility.
   Marketplace of 800+ third‑party apps scales functionality.
   Integrations & Marketplace: Extends core PMS with specialist tools. 
   Open API enables instant connection to accounting, AI, and distribution systems.
   Marketplace offers plug‑and‑play modules for loyalty, marketing, and POS.
   Seamless payment processing automates 100% of transactions.
2. Scenarios and Narratives
   Rapid On‑boarding of a Boutique Aparthotel: The property replaces a legacy system with Mews, achieving a two‑week remote deployment and 100% online onboarding. 
   Scenario: The hotel’s small IT team uses Mews Implementation Manager’s guided rollout, enabling staff to learn the interface in days. Guests now self‑check‑in via mobile, saving five minutes per arrival, while 60% of payments are processed automatically, freeing up admin time.
   Chain‑wide Revenue Optimisation: A hotel group leverages the Atomize RMS to adjust rates in real time across 30 locations. 
   Scenario: By feeding market data into the cloud‑native engine, the chain sees a 39% uplift in RevPAR and a 70% increase in average RevPAG through targeted upsells, all visible in a single analytics dashboard.
   Seamless Guest Journey for a Hostels Network: Hosts integrate Mews with smart‑lock and IoT platforms. 
   Scenario: Guests book online, receive a digital key on their phone, and can request housekeeping via the virtual concierge. The integration reduces front‑desk calls and improves NPS, while the marketplace supplies a one‑click loyalty app.
3. Strategic Benefits
   Operational Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks cuts staff workload, shortens check‑in times by five minutes and eliminates manual billing errors.
   Revenue Growth: Direct‑booking engine, dynamic pricing, and automated upsells deliver double‑digit percentage lifts in bookings and RevPAR.
   Scalability & Agility: Cloud‑native architecture supports rapid rollout to new properties without downtime, enabling multi‑property expansion.
   Guest Loyalty & Satisfaction: Unified guest journey and contact‑free services increase satisfaction scores and repeat bookings.
4. Core Functionalities
   Property Management System (PMS): Centralised reservation, billing, and housekeeping module with real‑time status updates.
   Revenue Management System (RMS – Atomize): AI‑driven pricing optimisation that reacts to market demand instantly.
   Booking Engine & Digital Key: Integrated front‑end for direct bookings and mobile key issuance.
   Mews Marketplace & Open API: Over 800 pre‑built integrations and a developer‑friendly API for custom extensions.
   Embedded Payments: PCI‑DSS‑compliant payment processing that automates 100% of transactions.
5. Customer Success Examples
   Locale (US aparthotel brand): Achieved 100% online onboarding, saved five minutes per check‑in, and drove >50% direct bookings with Mews Payments automating 60% of payments.
   HotelTechReport ranking: Named #1 Property Management System provider in the 2026 HotelTech Awards, with 93% of 773 surveyed hotels recommending Mews.
   Global hotel chain (12,500+ properties): Reported 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% RevPAR uplift, and 70% average RevPAG growth after switching to Mews.
6. Summary Table
| Category | Examples | Scenarios |
 --- 
| Front‑Desk Automation | Self‑service kiosks, digital keys, automated housekeeping alerts | Boutique aparthotel reduces check‑in time by 5 min, onboarding in 2 weeks |
| Revenue Management | Atomize RMS, upsell engine, direct‑booking portal | Chain sees +39 % RevPAR, +70 % RevPAG, +45 % direct bookings |
| Guest Experience | Online check‑in/out, mobile payments, guest journey platform | Hostel network offers contact‑free stays, reduces front‑desk calls |
| Multi‑Property Management | Single dashboard for 12,500+ properties, real‑time KPI reporting | Global brand scales operations without downtime |
| Integrations & Marketplace | 800+ third‑party apps, Open API, embedded payments | Hotels customise tech stack, automate 100 % of payments |
Sources:
   The Hospitality Management Software of the Future | Mews
   Locale customer story | Mews case study

supporting_evidence.title: With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is ...
supporting_evidence.snippet:
Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower). [...] The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.
   Tags
   Air Europa
   aizy
   baobab ventures
   Daniel Espejo
   elevenlabs
   EvoluteIQ
   GitHub
   kfund
   Klarna
   madrid
   Massive Dynamic
   Miguel Fernández
   Omnia
   pre-seed
   SylloTips
   Tandem
   Trade Republic
   Visionaries Club
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin [...] Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.

HomeFundingWith €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand out across AI platforms
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
October 16, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower).
“_AI assistants are becoming the front door to the internet_,” says Daniel Espejo, CEO and Co-founder of Omnia. “_Omnia gives teams the playbook and the agents to act on it. Moving from dashboards to done. Just like Cursor makes engineers 10× more productive, Omnia makes marketers 10× more effective on the channels that matter now_.”
Omnia’s pre-Seed round aligns with a growing wave of European investment into agentic and AI-driven marketing platforms in 2025.
Comparable early-stage rounds include Massive Dynamic (€3 million, Paris) and Tandem (€3.2 million, Paris), both developing AI-based orchestration tools. Further up the scale, Sweden’s EvoluteIQ secured €44 million to expand its enterprise agentic-AI suite, while Italy’s SylloTips (€4.2 million) and the Netherlands’ Aizy (€1.5 million) also closed funding to advance AI-based marketing and agent platforms.
Within this context, there haven’t been many comparable funding rounds in Spain, positioning Omnia as an early mover in Spain’s emerging agentic-AI marketing ecosystem.
“_As search shifts rapidly from browsers to AI engines and assistants, every brand will need an agentic strategy to stay visible,_” saidRobert Jaeckle, Partner at Visionaries Club. “_Omnia is pioneering this space by building the agentic platform that turns visibility into action for marketeers. We’re so excited to partner with Daniel and Miguel as they define this new category for Europe and beyond._”
Omnia was founded in 2025 by Daniel Espejo (CEO), formerly at Klarna where he launched markets and scaled products used by millions, and Miguel Fernández (CTO), a platform engineer with experience at GitHub and Fastly.
Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.
Since launch, Omnia has been adopted by brands including Personio, ElevenLabs, Embat, Línea Directa, Ironhack, Air Europa, Praktika.ai , Trade Republic, Pleo and HappyRobot. ​
Also by marketing agencies like MIO group, Elogia, Product Hackers and Everybody. Early customers are allegedly reporting up to 3× visibility gains across AI channels.
“_Omnia has been key to our growth – helping us understand what potential clients are searching for, how well we’re positioned, and how to capture leads from AI across different markets,_” shared Adrian Varez, Head of Growth at Happy Robot.
The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.

HomeFundingMadrid-based startup Shakers raises €14 million to help businesses integrate teams of...
FundingSpain-Startups
Madrid-based startup Shakers raises €14 million to help businesses integrate teams of specialised talent with AI
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
HRTech startup Shakers, a Spanish workforce management platform connecting companies with tech freelancers, today announced its €14 million Series A funding round in order to expand double its headcount.
The funding round was led by Partech, with further investment from KFund, Brighteye Ventures, Athos Capital and Wayra.
Héctor Mata, Shakers CEO, said: “The convergence of AI and independent talent is not a trend – it’s a new operating model. Demand for specialised technical talent is soaring, but the traditional outsourcing model hasn’t evolved in over decades. With Shakers, we are setting a new standard for hiring: our platform acts as the new operating system for performance-driven freelancers — giving them the same benefits as traditional roles, while offering companies the agility to innovate faster. As we expand across Europe, we’re building the infrastructure layer that powers the AI-infused workplace of the future.”
Founded in 2021, Shakers is a workforce orchestration platform that connects businesses with specialised tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents. Founded by four serial entrepreneurs – Héctor Mata (CEO), Nico de Luis (COO), Adrián de Pedro (CPO) and Jaime Castillo (CRO) – with backgrounds in AI and software development, and first-hand experience in both finding freelance work and hiring talent.
With over 10,000 vetted freelancers and 450+ client companies including Microsoft, Inditex and Telefónica, Shakers aims to empower a new generation of performance-driven freeworkers with integrated services such as banking, insurance and productivity tools – all within its all-in-one Freelancer OS.
The company has grown over 6x in revenue over the past year, and is aiming to continue on its growth trajectory. 60% of their revenue reportedly comes from recurring customers and 30% of revenue generated organically from other European markets.
Shakers envisions a future of work where businesses can easily onboard specialised teams of tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents to collaborate, experiment, and drive new technological capabilities via complex and long-term projects. The platform empowers businesses and freelancers to automate their most “frustrating” workflow challenges and upgrade their productivity skills, promoting quick and effective results.
According to data provided by Shakers, this comes at a time when the traditional IT outsourcing landscape is being reshaped, with the European market projected to grow from €180 billion to over €280 billion by 2030 as demand shifts toward more flexible, high-skill talent solutions.
Shakers plans to double its team size in the next year to facilitate their growth, while expanding its AI agent integrations for freelancers and businesses. By 2027, Shakers will reportedly have orchestrated over 10,000 hybrid projects.
Simone Riva, Partech Senior Principal, said: “At Partech, we believe that while AI is reshaping the way we work, exceptional talent will always be at the heart of the future of work. From our very first conversation, it was clear the Shakers team is building something special — a fast, intuitive platform tailored to the flexible workforce of tomorrow. They’re redefining how companies find, hire, and collaborate with top talent. We’re proud to back this visionary team and are excited to support them as they scale across Spain, Europe, and beyond.”
As part of Shakers’ community of 10,000+ tech freelancers, they will enjoy the same benefits as traditional employment, including embedded services for banking, insurance, productivity and more, alongside the flexibility, freedom and incentives of self-employment.
According to Shakers, their technology goes beyond just sourcing – it validates talent end-to-end, assessing both hard and soft skills, experience, and cultural fit through more than 1,000 data points.
Integrated with Shakers Verified, their pre-vetting tool, the platform eliminates the need for early interviews and technical tests, delivering ready-to-work talent from day one. This means that companies can potentially go from idea to fully assembled team in minutes.

Executive Summary
Value Proposition & Problem: Mews offers a cloud‑native property management system that eliminates manual, fragmented hotel operations, enabling hoteliers to focus on guest experience and profitability.
Product / Service: A unified hospitality platform covering PMS, payments, revenue management, bookings, and marketplace integrations, with AI‑driven automation and an open API.
Market Stage & Traction: Over 12,500 properties in 85 countries use Mews, representing a customer base growth of 85% year‑over‑year and a transaction volume of $19.7 billion in 2025.
Achievements: Recognized as Best PMS (2024‑2026) and Best POS (2026) by Hotel Tech Report, 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% rise in RevPAR, and 70% average RevPAG uplift for customers.

Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World's Leading Hospitality Operating System
Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Published January 22, 2026 Press EQT Growth leads Series D funding round to accelerate the AI-native future of hotels globally, with participation from new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Investment values Mews at $2.5 billion Mews’ Platform Transaction Volume reached $19.7 billion in 2025 Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: [New York, 22 January 2026] – Mews , the operating system for hospitality, today announced it has raised $300 million in a Series D funding round led by EQT Growth , with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners , and participation from existing investors Kinnevik , Battery Ventures and Tiger Global . This investment marks a defining moment for hospitality technology and values the company at $2.5 billion. Following a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%, this fundraise establishes Mews as the leading hospitality tech provider for hotels of all sizes. In 2025, the company achieved: Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: - A customer base of 15,000, with 132,000+ monthly active hoteliers in 85 countries - 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk 1 - Platform Transaction Volumes of $19.7 billion - Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% - Created more than half a billion ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces 2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations “With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry,” said Matt Welle, CEO of Mews. “We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in artificial intelligence, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed - enabling properties to: - Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation - Optimize revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior - Scale business without the debt of legacy tech - Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “Hospitality is the business of experiences,” said Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry.” A new standard for a global industry Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale,” said Kirk Lepke, Partner at EQT Growth. “Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Mews will also accelerate the expansion of Mews Payments and its broader fintech infrastructure, embedding commerce into the core of hotel operations and positioning Mews as the connector between experience and revenue. In addition, the company will continue its ambitious international expansion across its primary geographies of North America and Europe, as well as expanding into new markets. ... Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: The investment comes shortly after the completion of Mews’ fourteenth acquisition, DataChat, the leading generative AI analytics platform. In January 2026, Mews was named the #1 Property Management System for the third year in a row and #1 Hotel Point of Sale System in the world by customers and reviewers of Hotel Tech Report, a reflection of its ability to lead both the operational and commercial layers of hospitality. The funding round is subject to customary regulatory closing conditions. 1 A self‑service check‑in and check‑out kiosk that allows guests to handle arrival and departure themselves. 2 Mews’ multi‑space management capability that lets a property manage and sell any kind of space (not just rooms) directly in the property management system (PMS). Funding Netherland-Startups Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content:  By David Cendon Garcia January 22, 2026 Share Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Facebook Twitter WhatsApp Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Linkedin Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content:  Amsterdam-based Mews , an OS for hospitality, today announced it has raised €255 million ($300 million) in a Series D funding round in order to further invest in AI, embed agent-driven systems to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, improve the guest experience, and accelerate how products are built and deployed The round was led by EQT Growth, with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners, and participation from existing investors Kinnevik, Battery Ventures and Tiger Global – valuing the company at €2.1 billion ($2.5 billion). Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry, ” says Matt Welle , CEO of Mews. “ We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling .” In the context of recent European hospitality and HotelTech funding activity, Amsterdam-based Mews’ €255 million Series D represents one of the largest pure-play software raises in the sector. ... Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Taken together, these announcements indicate that roughly €695 million has been committed to European hospitality and adjacent HotelTech models in 2025/2026, with Mews’ raise accounting for a substantial share of that total. Also of note, is that in March 2024 Mews also raised €101 million to help hoteliers embrace cloud technology . “ Hospitality is the business of experiences, ” adds Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “ The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry. ” Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Founded in 2012, Mews an OS for hospitality, unifying workflows across revenue, operations and the guest journey so teams can automate the mundane and focus on memorable guest experiences. Powering 15,000 customers across 85 countries, the Mews platform spans PMS, POS, RMS, Housekeeping and Payments, helping hoteliers move from property management to profit management. This new funding follows a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%. In 2025, the company achieved: 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk1 Platform Transaction Volumes of €16.7 billion ($19.7 billion) Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% Created more than €457 million ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in AI, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed – enabling properties to: Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation Optimise revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior Scale business without the debt of legacy tech Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale ,” said Kirk Lepke , Partner at EQT Growth. “ Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews .” Mews will also accelerate the expansion of Mews Payments and its broader FinTech infrastructure, embedding commerce into the core of hotel operations and positioning Mews as the connector between experience and revenue. The investment comes shortly after the completion of Mews’ fourteenth acquisition, DataChat, a generative AI analytics platform. Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ Matt and Richard have built a category-defining platform with the depth, pace of innovation and global reach required by modern hospitality ,” said Laura Connell , Partner at Atomico. “ The most ambitious builders in hospitality are focused on delivering ever-improving experiences for their end consumers, and they need technology that can keep pace with rising expectations around speed, service and personalisation. Mews is the key enabler for the future of hospitality, and the team is well on their way to building a generational company .” Share Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Together, we are creating a foundation for the next generation of hoteliers to thrive, not just today, but in a rapidly evolving industry that demands forward-thinking, scalable solutions.” ...
