What are the use cases of Mews?Copy Link
1. Use Cases by Category
   Front‑Desk Automation: Streamlines check‑in, check‑out and housekeeping communication. 
   Self‑service kiosks reduce check‑in time by 5 minutes.
   Digital key issuance eliminates front‑desk bottlenecks.
   Automated housekeeping notifications cut call volume.
   Revenue Management & Upselling: Optimizes pricing and drives ancillary sales. 
   Dynamic pricing engine (Atomize) boosts RevPAR by 39%.
   Embedded upsell offers increase average RevPAG by 70%.
   Direct‑booking engine lifts bookings by 45%.
   Guest Experience Personalisation: Creates seamless, end‑to‑end journeys. 
   Mews Guest Journey platform unifies booking, check‑in, in‑stay services.
   Online check‑in/out and mobile payments cut guest wait times.
   Smart‑device integrations (e.g., Operto) enable contact‑free stays.
   Multi‑Property & Chain Management: Centralises operations across dozens of locations. 
   Single dashboard controls 12,500+ properties worldwide.
   Unified reporting provides real‑time KPI visibility.
   Marketplace of 800+ third‑party apps scales functionality.
   Integrations & Marketplace: Extends core PMS with specialist tools. 
   Open API enables instant connection to accounting, AI, and distribution systems.
   Marketplace offers plug‑and‑play modules for loyalty, marketing, and POS.
   Seamless payment processing automates 100% of transactions.
2. Scenarios and Narratives
   Rapid On‑boarding of a Boutique Aparthotel: The property replaces a legacy system with Mews, achieving a two‑week remote deployment and 100% online onboarding. 
   Scenario: The hotel’s small IT team uses Mews Implementation Manager’s guided rollout, enabling staff to learn the interface in days. Guests now self‑check‑in via mobile, saving five minutes per arrival, while 60% of payments are processed automatically, freeing up admin time.
   Chain‑wide Revenue Optimisation: A hotel group leverages the Atomize RMS to adjust rates in real time across 30 locations. 
   Scenario: By feeding market data into the cloud‑native engine, the chain sees a 39% uplift in RevPAR and a 70% increase in average RevPAG through targeted upsells, all visible in a single analytics dashboard.
   Seamless Guest Journey for a Hostels Network: Hosts integrate Mews with smart‑lock and IoT platforms. 
   Scenario: Guests book online, receive a digital key on their phone, and can request housekeeping via the virtual concierge. The integration reduces front‑desk calls and improves NPS, while the marketplace supplies a one‑click loyalty app.
3. Strategic Benefits
   Operational Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks cuts staff workload, shortens check‑in times by five minutes and eliminates manual billing errors.
   Revenue Growth: Direct‑booking engine, dynamic pricing, and automated upsells deliver double‑digit percentage lifts in bookings and RevPAR.
   Scalability & Agility: Cloud‑native architecture supports rapid rollout to new properties without downtime, enabling multi‑property expansion.
   Guest Loyalty & Satisfaction: Unified guest journey and contact‑free services increase satisfaction scores and repeat bookings.
4. Core Functionalities
   Property Management System (PMS): Centralised reservation, billing, and housekeeping module with real‑time status updates.
   Revenue Management System (RMS – Atomize): AI‑driven pricing optimisation that reacts to market demand instantly.
   Booking Engine & Digital Key: Integrated front‑end for direct bookings and mobile key issuance.
   Mews Marketplace & Open API: Over 800 pre‑built integrations and a developer‑friendly API for custom extensions.
   Embedded Payments: PCI‑DSS‑compliant payment processing that automates 100% of transactions.
5. Customer Success Examples
   Locale (US aparthotel brand): Achieved 100% online onboarding, saved five minutes per check‑in, and drove >50% direct bookings with Mews Payments automating 60% of payments.
   HotelTechReport ranking: Named #1 Property Management System provider in the 2026 HotelTech Awards, with 93% of 773 surveyed hotels recommending Mews.
   Global hotel chain (12,500+ properties): Reported 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% RevPAR uplift, and 70% average RevPAG growth after switching to Mews.
6. Summary Table
| Category | Examples | Scenarios |
 --- 
| Front‑Desk Automation | Self‑service kiosks, digital keys, automated housekeeping alerts | Boutique aparthotel reduces check‑in time by 5 min, onboarding in 2 weeks |
| Revenue Management | Atomize RMS, upsell engine, direct‑booking portal | Chain sees +39 % RevPAR, +70 % RevPAG, +45 % direct bookings |
| Guest Experience | Online check‑in/out, mobile payments, guest journey platform | Hostel network offers contact‑free stays, reduces front‑desk calls |
| Multi‑Property Management | Single dashboard for 12,500+ properties, real‑time KPI reporting | Global brand scales operations without downtime |
| Integrations & Marketplace | 800+ third‑party apps, Open API, embedded payments | Hotels customise tech stack, automate 100 % of payments |

HomeFundingWith €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand out across AI platforms
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
October 16, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower).
“_AI assistants are becoming the front door to the internet_,” says Daniel Espejo, CEO and Co-founder of Omnia. “_Omnia gives teams the playbook and the agents to act on it. Moving from dashboards to done. Just like Cursor makes engineers 10× more productive, Omnia makes marketers 10× more effective on the channels that matter now_.”
Omnia’s pre-Seed round aligns with a growing wave of European investment into agentic and AI-driven marketing platforms in 2025.
Comparable early-stage rounds include Massive Dynamic (€3 million, Paris) and Tandem (€3.2 million, Paris), both developing AI-based orchestration tools. Further up the scale, Sweden’s EvoluteIQ secured €44 million to expand its enterprise agentic-AI suite, while Italy’s SylloTips (€4.2 million) and the Netherlands’ Aizy (€1.5 million) also closed funding to advance AI-based marketing and agent platforms.
Within this context, there haven’t been many comparable funding rounds in Spain, positioning Omnia as an early mover in Spain’s emerging agentic-AI marketing ecosystem.
“_As search shifts rapidly from browsers to AI engines and assistants, every brand will need an agentic strategy to stay visible,_” saidRobert Jaeckle, Partner at Visionaries Club. “_Omnia is pioneering this space by building the agentic platform that turns visibility into action for marketeers. We’re so excited to partner with Daniel and Miguel as they define this new category for Europe and beyond._”
Omnia was founded in 2025 by Daniel Espejo (CEO), formerly at Klarna where he launched markets and scaled products used by millions, and Miguel Fernández (CTO), a platform engineer with experience at GitHub and Fastly.
Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.
Since launch, Omnia has been adopted by brands including Personio, ElevenLabs, Embat, Línea Directa, Ironhack, Air Europa, Praktika.ai , Trade Republic, Pleo and HappyRobot. ​
Also by marketing agencies like MIO group, Elogia, Product Hackers and Everybody. Early customers are allegedly reporting up to 3× visibility gains across AI channels.
“_Omnia has been key to our growth – helping us understand what potential clients are searching for, how well we’re positioned, and how to capture leads from AI across different markets,_” shared Adrian Varez, Head of Growth at Happy Robot.
The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.

HomeFundingMadrid-based startup Shakers raises €14 million to help businesses integrate teams of...
FundingSpain-Startups
Madrid-based startup Shakers raises €14 million to help businesses integrate teams of specialised talent with AI
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
HRTech startup Shakers, a Spanish workforce management platform connecting companies with tech freelancers, today announced its €14 million Series A funding round in order to expand double its headcount.
The funding round was led by Partech, with further investment from KFund, Brighteye Ventures, Athos Capital and Wayra.
Héctor Mata, Shakers CEO, said: “The convergence of AI and independent talent is not a trend – it’s a new operating model. Demand for specialised technical talent is soaring, but the traditional outsourcing model hasn’t evolved in over decades. With Shakers, we are setting a new standard for hiring: our platform acts as the new operating system for performance-driven freelancers — giving them the same benefits as traditional roles, while offering companies the agility to innovate faster. As we expand across Europe, we’re building the infrastructure layer that powers the AI-infused workplace of the future.”
Founded in 2021, Shakers is a workforce orchestration platform that connects businesses with specialised tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents. Founded by four serial entrepreneurs – Héctor Mata (CEO), Nico de Luis (COO), Adrián de Pedro (CPO) and Jaime Castillo (CRO) – with backgrounds in AI and software development, and first-hand experience in both finding freelance work and hiring talent.
With over 10,000 vetted freelancers and 450+ client companies including Microsoft, Inditex and Telefónica, Shakers aims to empower a new generation of performance-driven freeworkers with integrated services such as banking, insurance and productivity tools – all within its all-in-one Freelancer OS.
The company has grown over 6x in revenue over the past year, and is aiming to continue on its growth trajectory. 60% of their revenue reportedly comes from recurring customers and 30% of revenue generated organically from other European markets.
Shakers envisions a future of work where businesses can easily onboard specialised teams of tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents to collaborate, experiment, and drive new technological capabilities via complex and long-term projects. The platform empowers businesses and freelancers to automate their most “frustrating” workflow challenges and upgrade their productivity skills, promoting quick and effective results.
According to data provided by Shakers, this comes at a time when the traditional IT outsourcing landscape is being reshaped, with the European market projected to grow from €180 billion to over €280 billion by 2030 as demand shifts toward more flexible, high-skill talent solutions.
Shakers plans to double its team size in the next year to facilitate their growth, while expanding its AI agent integrations for freelancers and businesses. By 2027, Shakers will reportedly have orchestrated over 10,000 hybrid projects.
Simone Riva, Partech Senior Principal, said: “At Partech, we believe that while AI is reshaping the way we work, exceptional talent will always be at the heart of the future of work. From our very first conversation, it was clear the Shakers team is building something special — a fast, intuitive platform tailored to the flexible workforce of tomorrow. They’re redefining how companies find, hire, and collaborate with top talent. We’re proud to back this visionary team and are excited to support them as they scale across Spain, Europe, and beyond.”
As part of Shakers’ community of 10,000+ tech freelancers, they will enjoy the same benefits as traditional employment, including embedded services for banking, insurance, productivity and more, alongside the flexibility, freedom and incentives of self-employment.
According to Shakers, their technology goes beyond just sourcing – it validates talent end-to-end, assessing both hard and soft skills, experience, and cultural fit through more than 1,000 data points.
Integrated with Shakers Verified, their pre-vetting tool, the platform eliminates the need for early interviews and technical tests, delivering ready-to-work talent from day one. This means that companies can potentially go from idea to fully assembled team in minutes.

Executive Summary
Value Proposition & Problem: Mews offers a cloud‑native property management system that eliminates manual, fragmented hotel operations, enabling hoteliers to focus on guest experience and profitability.
Product / Service: A unified hospitality platform covering PMS, payments, revenue management, bookings, and marketplace integrations, with AI‑driven automation and an open API.
Market Stage & Traction: Over 12,500 properties in 85 countries use Mews, representing a customer base growth of 85% year‑over‑year and a transaction volume of $19.7 billion in 2025.
Achievements: Recognized as Best PMS (2024‑2026) and Best POS (2026) by Hotel Tech Report, 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% rise in RevPAR, and 70% average RevPAG uplift for customers.

Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World's Leading Hospitality Operating System
Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Published January 22, 2026 Press EQT Growth leads Series D funding round to accelerate the AI-native future of hotels globally, with participation from new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Investment values Mews at $2.5 billion Mews’ Platform Transaction Volume reached $19.7 billion in 2025 Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: [New York, 22 January 2026] – Mews , the operating system for hospitality, today announced it has raised $300 million in a Series D funding round led by EQT Growth , with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners , and participation from existing investors Kinnevik , Battery Ventures and Tiger Global . This investment marks a defining moment for hospitality technology and values the company at $2.5 billion. Following a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%, this fundraise establishes Mews as the leading hospitality tech provider for hotels of all sizes. In 2025, the company achieved: Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: - A customer base of 15,000, with 132,000+ monthly active hoteliers in 85 countries - 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk 1 - Platform Transaction Volumes of $19.7 billion - Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% - Created more than half a billion ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces 2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations “With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry,” said Matt Welle, CEO of Mews. “We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in artificial intelligence, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed - enabling properties to: - Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation - Optimize revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior - Scale business without the debt of legacy tech - Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “Hospitality is the business of experiences,” said Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry.” A new standard for a global industry Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale,” said Kirk Lepke, Partner at EQT Growth. “Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Mews will also accelerate the expansion of Mews Payments and its broader fintech infrastructure, embedding commerce into the core of hotel operations and positioning Mews as the connector between experience and revenue. In addition, the company will continue its ambitious international expansion across its primary geographies of North America and Europe, as well as expanding into new markets. ... Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: The investment comes shortly after the completion of Mews’ fourteenth acquisition, DataChat, the leading generative AI analytics platform. In January 2026, Mews was named the #1 Property Management System for the third year in a row and #1 Hotel Point of Sale System in the world by customers and reviewers of Hotel Tech Report, a reflection of its ability to lead both the operational and commercial layers of hospitality. The funding round is subject to customary regulatory closing conditions. 1 A self‑service check‑in and check‑out kiosk that allows guests to handle arrival and departure themselves. 2 Mews’ multi‑space management capability that lets a property manage and sell any kind of space (not just rooms) directly in the property management system (PMS). Funding Netherland-Startups Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content:  By David Cendon Garcia January 22, 2026 Share Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Facebook Twitter WhatsApp Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Linkedin Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content:  Amsterdam-based Mews , an OS for hospitality, today announced it has raised €255 million ($300 million) in a Series D funding round in order to further invest in AI, embed agent-driven systems to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, improve the guest experience, and accelerate how products are built and deployed The round was led by EQT Growth, with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners, and participation from existing investors Kinnevik, Battery Ventures and Tiger Global – valuing the company at €2.1 billion ($2.5 billion). Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry, ” says Matt Welle , CEO of Mews. “ We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling .” In the context of recent European hospitality and HotelTech funding activity, Amsterdam-based Mews’ €255 million Series D represents one of the largest pure-play software raises in the sector. ... Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Taken together, these announcements indicate that roughly €695 million has been committed to European hospitality and adjacent HotelTech models in 2025/2026, with Mews’ raise accounting for a substantial share of that total. Also of note, is that in March 2024 Mews also raised €101 million to help hoteliers embrace cloud technology . “ Hospitality is the business of experiences, ” adds Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “ The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry. ” Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Founded in 2012, Mews an OS for hospitality, unifying workflows across revenue, operations and the guest journey so teams can automate the mundane and focus on memorable guest experiences. Powering 15,000 customers across 85 countries, the Mews platform spans PMS, POS, RMS, Housekeeping and Payments, helping hoteliers move from property management to profit management. This new funding follows a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%. In 2025, the company achieved: 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk1 Platform Transaction Volumes of €16.7 billion ($19.7 billion) Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% Created more than €457 million ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in AI, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed – enabling properties to: Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation Optimise revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior Scale business without the debt of legacy tech Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale ,” said Kirk Lepke , Partner at EQT Growth. “ Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews .” Mews will also accelerate the expansion of Mews Payments and its broader FinTech infrastructure, embedding commerce into the core of hotel operations and positioning Mews as the connector between experience and revenue. The investment comes shortly after the completion of Mews’ fourteenth acquisition, DataChat, a generative AI analytics platform. Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ Matt and Richard have built a category-defining platform with the depth, pace of innovation and global reach required by modern hospitality ,” said Laura Connell , Partner at Atomico. “ The most ambitious builders in hospitality are focused on delivering ever-improving experiences for their end consumers, and they need technology that can keep pace with rising expectations around speed, service and personalisation. Mews is the key enabler for the future of hospitality, and the team is well on their way to building a generational company .” Share Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... What are the use cases of Mews?Copy Link
1. Use Cases by Category
   Front‑Desk Automation: Streamlines check‑in, check‑out and housekeeping communication. 
   Self‑service kiosks reduce check‑in time by 5 minutes.
   Digital key issuance eliminates front‑desk bottlenecks.
   Automated housekeeping notifications cut call volume.
   Revenue Management & Upselling: Optimizes pricing and drives ancillary sales. 
   Dynamic pricing engine (Atomize) boosts RevPAR by 39%.
   Embedded upsell offers increase average RevPAG by 70%.
   Direct‑booking engine lifts bookings by 45%.
   Guest Experience Personalisation: Creates seamless, end‑to‑end journeys. 
   Mews Guest Journey platform unifies booking, check‑in, in‑stay services.
   Online check‑in/out and mobile payments cut guest wait times.
   Smart‑device integrations (e.g., Operto) enable contact‑free stays.
   Multi‑Property & Chain Management: Centralises operations across dozens of locations. 
   Single dashboard controls 12,500+ properties worldwide.
   Unified reporting provides real‑time KPI visibility.
   Marketplace of 800+ third‑party apps scales functionality.
   Integrations & Marketplace: Extends core PMS with specialist tools. 
   Open API enables instant connection to accounting, AI, and distribution systems.
   Marketplace offers plug‑and‑play modules for loyalty, marketing, and POS.
   Seamless payment processing automates 100% of transactions.
2. Scenarios and Narratives
   Rapid On‑boarding of a Boutique Aparthotel: The property replaces a legacy system with Mews, achieving a two‑week remote deployment and 100% online onboarding. 
   Scenario: The hotel’s small IT team uses Mews Implementation Manager’s guided rollout, enabling staff to learn the interface in days. Guests now self‑check‑in via mobile, saving five minutes per arrival, while 60% of payments are processed automatically, freeing up admin time.
   Chain‑wide Revenue Optimisation: A hotel group leverages the Atomize RMS to adjust rates in real time across 30 locations. 
   Scenario: By feeding market data into the cloud‑native engine, the chain sees a 39% uplift in RevPAR and a 70% increase in average RevPAG through targeted upsells, all visible in a single analytics dashboard.
   Seamless Guest Journey for a Hostels Network: Hosts integrate Mews with smart‑lock and IoT platforms. 
   Scenario: Guests book online, receive a digital key on their phone, and can request housekeeping via the virtual concierge. The integration reduces front‑desk calls and improves NPS, while the marketplace supplies a one‑click loyalty app.
3. Strategic Benefits
   Operational Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks cuts staff workload, shortens check‑in times by five minutes and eliminates manual billing errors.
   Revenue Growth: Direct‑booking engine, dynamic pricing, and automated upsells deliver double‑digit percentage lifts in bookings and RevPAR.
   Scalability & Agility: Cloud‑native architecture supports rapid rollout to new properties without downtime, enabling multi‑property expansion.
   Guest Loyalty & Satisfaction: Unified guest journey and contact‑free services increase satisfaction scores and repeat bookings.
4. Core Functionalities
   Property Management System (PMS): Centralised reservation, billing, and housekeeping module with real‑time status updates.
   Revenue Management System (RMS – Atomize): AI‑driven pricing optimisation that reacts to market demand instantly.
   Booking Engine & Digital Key: Integrated front‑end for direct bookings and mobile key issuance.
   Mews Marketplace & Open API: Over 800 pre‑built integrations and a developer‑friendly API for custom extensions.
   Embedded Payments: PCI‑DSS‑compliant payment processing that automates 100% of transactions.
5. Customer Success Examples
   Locale (US aparthotel brand): Achieved 100% online onboarding, saved five minutes per check‑in, and drove >50% direct bookings with Mews Payments automating 60% of payments.
   HotelTechReport ranking: Named #1 Property Management System provider in the 2026 HotelTech Awards, with 93% of 773 surveyed hotels recommending Mews.
   Global hotel chain (12,500+ properties): Reported 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% RevPAR uplift, and 70% average RevPAG growth after switching to Mews.
6. Summary Table
| Category | Examples | Scenarios |
 --- 
| Front‑Desk Automation | Self‑service kiosks, digital keys, automated housekeeping alerts | Boutique aparthotel reduces check‑in time by 5 min, onboarding in 2 weeks |
| Revenue Management | Atomize RMS, upsell engine, direct‑booking portal | Chain sees +39 % RevPAR, +70 % RevPAG, +45 % direct bookings |
| Guest Experience | Online check‑in/out, mobile payments, guest journey platform | Hostel network offers contact‑free stays, reduces front‑desk calls |
| Multi‑Property Management | Single dashboard for 12,500+ properties, real‑time KPI reporting | Global brand scales operations without downtime |
| Integrations & Marketplace | 800+ third‑party apps, Open API, embedded payments | Hotels customise tech stack, automate 100 % of payments |

HomeFundingWith €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand out across AI platforms
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
October 16, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower).
“_AI assistants are becoming the front door to the internet_,” says Daniel Espejo, CEO and Co-founder of Omnia. “_Omnia gives teams the playbook and the agents to act on it. Moving from dashboards to done. Just like Cursor makes engineers 10× more productive, Omnia makes marketers 10× more effective on the channels that matter now_.”
Omnia’s pre-Seed round aligns with a growing wave of European investment into agentic and AI-driven marketing platforms in 2025.
Comparable early-stage rounds include Massive Dynamic (€3 million, Paris) and Tandem (€3.2 million, Paris), both developing AI-based orchestration tools. Further up the scale, Sweden’s EvoluteIQ secured €44 million to expand its enterprise agentic-AI suite, while Italy’s SylloTips (€4.2 million) and the Netherlands’ Aizy (€1.5 million) also closed funding to advance AI-based marketing and agent platforms.
Within this context, there haven’t been many comparable funding rounds in Spain, positioning Omnia as an early mover in Spain’s emerging agentic-AI marketing ecosystem.
“_As search shifts rapidly from browsers to AI engines and assistants, every brand will need an agentic strategy to stay visible,_” saidRobert Jaeckle, Partner at Visionaries Club. “_Omnia is pioneering this space by building the agentic platform that turns visibility into action for marketeers. We’re so excited to partner with Daniel and Miguel as they define this new category for Europe and beyond._”
Omnia was founded in 2025 by Daniel Espejo (CEO), formerly at Klarna where he launched markets and scaled products used by millions, and Miguel Fernández (CTO), a platform engineer with experience at GitHub and Fastly.
Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.
Since launch, Omnia has been adopted by brands including Personio, ElevenLabs, Embat, Línea Directa, Ironhack, Air Europa, Praktika.ai , Trade Republic, Pleo and HappyRobot. ​
Also by marketing agencies like MIO group, Elogia, Product Hackers and Everybody. Early customers are allegedly reporting up to 3× visibility gains across AI channels.
“_Omnia has been key to our growth – helping us understand what potential clients are searching for, how well we’re positioned, and how to capture leads from AI across different markets,_” shared Adrian Varez, Head of Growth at Happy Robot.
The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.

FundingSpain-Startups
Madrid-based startup Shakers raises €14 million to help businesses integrate teams of specialised talent with AI
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
HRTech startup Shakers, a Spanish workforce management platform connecting companies with tech freelancers, today announced its €14 million Series A funding round in order to expand double its headcount.
The funding round was led by Partech, with further investment from KFund, Brighteye Ventures, Athos Capital and Wayra.
Héctor Mata, Shakers CEO, said: “The convergence of AI and independent talent is not a trend – it’s a new operating model. Demand for specialised technical talent is soaring, but the traditional outsourcing model hasn’t evolved in over decades. With Shakers, we are setting a new standard for hiring: our platform acts as the new operating system for performance-driven freelancers — giving them the same benefits as traditional roles, while offering companies the agility to innovate faster. As we expand across Europe, we’re building the infrastructure layer that powers the AI-infused workplace of the future.”
Founded in 2021, Shakers is a workforce orchestration platform that connects businesses with specialised tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents. Founded by four serial entrepreneurs – Héctor Mata (CEO), Nico de Luis (COO), Adrián de Pedro (CPO) and Jaime Castillo (CRO) – with backgrounds in AI and software development, and first-hand experience in both finding freelance work and hiring talent.
With over 10,000 vetted freelancers and 450+ client companies including Microsoft, Inditex and Telefónica, Shakers aims to empower a new generation of performance-driven freeworkers with integrated services such as banking, insurance and productivity tools – all within its all-in-one Freelancer OS.
The company has grown over 6x in revenue over the past year, and is aiming to continue on its growth trajectory. 60% of their revenue reportedly comes from recurring customers and 30% of revenue generated organically from other European markets.
Shakers envisions a future of work where businesses can easily onboard specialised teams of tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents to collaborate, experiment, and drive new technological capabilities via complex and long-term projects. The platform empowers businesses and freelancers to automate their most “frustrating” workflow challenges and upgrade their productivity skills, promoting quick and effective results.
According to data provided by Shakers, this comes at a time when the traditional IT outsourcing landscape is being reshaped, with the European market projected to grow from €180 billion to over €280 billion by 2030 as demand shifts toward more flexible, high-skill talent solutions.
Shakers plans to double its team size in the next year to facilitate their growth, while expanding its AI agent integrations for freelancers and businesses. By 2027, Shakers will reportedly have orchestrated over 10,000 hybrid projects.
Simone Riva, Partech Senior Principal, said: “At Partech, we believe that while AI is reshaping the way we work, exceptional talent will always be at the heart of the future of work. From our very first conversation, it was clear the Shakers team is building something special — a fast, intuitive platform tailored to the flexible workforce of tomorrow. They’re redefining how companies find, hire, and collaborate with top talent. We’re proud to back this visionary team and are excited to support them as they scale across Spain, Europe, and beyond.”
As part of Shakers’ community of 10,000+ tech freelancers, they will enjoy the same benefits as traditional employment, including embedded services for banking, insurance, productivity and more, alongside the flexibility, freedom and incentives of self-employment.
According to Shakers, their technology goes beyond just sourcing – it validates talent end-to-end, assessing both hard and soft skills, experience, and cultural fit through more than 1,000 data points.
Integrated with Shakers Verified, their pre-vetting tool, the platform eliminates the need for early interviews and technical tests, delivering ready-to-work talent from day one. This means that companies can potentially go from idea to fully assembled team in minutes.

Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World's Leading Hospitality Operating System
Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Published January 22, 2026 Press EQT Growth leads Series D funding round to accelerate the AI-native future of hotels globally, with participation from new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Investment values Mews at $2.5 billion Mews’ Platform Transaction Volume reached $19.7 billion in 2025 Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: [New York, 22 January 2026] – Mews , the operating system for hospitality, today announced it has raised $300 million in a Series D funding round led by EQT Growth , with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners , and participation from existing investors Kinnevik , Battery Ventures and Tiger Global . This investment marks a defining moment for hospitality technology and values the company at $2.5 billion. Following a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%, this fundraise establishes Mews as the leading hospitality tech provider for hotels of all sizes. In 2025, the company achieved: Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: - A customer base of 15,000, with 132,000+ monthly active hoteliers in 85 countries - 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk 1 - Platform Transaction Volumes of $19.7 billion - Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% - Created more than half a billion ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces 2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations “With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry,” said Matt Welle, CEO of Mews. “We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in artificial intelligence, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed - enabling properties to: - Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation - Optimize revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior - Scale business without the debt of legacy tech - Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “Hospitality is the business of experiences,” said Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry.” A new standard for a global industry Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale,” said Kirk Lepke, Partner at EQT Growth. “Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Mews will also accelerate the expansion of Mews Payments and its broader fintech infrastructure, embedding commerce into the core of hotel operations and positioning Mews as the connector between experience and revenue. In addition, the company will continue its ambitious international expansion across its primary geographies of North America and Europe, as well as expanding into new markets. ... Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: The investment comes shortly after the completion of Mews’ fourteenth acquisition, DataChat, the leading generative AI analytics platform. In January 2026, Mews was named the #1 Property Management System for the third year in a row and #1 Hotel Point of Sale System in the world by customers and reviewers of Hotel Tech Report, a reflection of its ability to lead both the operational and commercial layers of hospitality. Funding Netherland-Startups Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content:  By David Cendon Garcia January 22, 2026 Share Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Facebook Twitter WhatsApp Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Linkedin Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content:  Amsterdam-based Mews , an OS for hospitality, today announced it has raised €255 million ($300 million) in a Series D funding round in order to further invest in AI, embed agent-driven systems to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, improve the guest experience, and accelerate how products are built and deployed The round was led by EQT Growth, with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners, and participation from existing investors Kinnevik, Battery Ventures and Tiger Global – valuing the company at €2.1 billion ($2.5 billion). Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry, ” says Matt Welle , CEO of Mews. “ We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling .” In the context of recent European hospitality and HotelTech funding activity, Amsterdam-based Mews’ €255 million Series D represents one of the largest pure-play software raises in the sector. ... Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Taken together, these announcements indicate that roughly €695 million has been committed to European hospitality and adjacent HotelTech models in 2025/2026, with Mews’ raise accounting for a substantial share of that total. Also of note, is that in March 2024 Mews also raised €101 million to help hoteliers embrace cloud technology . “ Hospitality is the business of experiences, ” adds Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “ The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry. ” Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Founded in 2012, Mews an OS for hospitality, unifying workflows across revenue, operations and the guest journey so teams can automate the mundane and focus on memorable guest experiences. Powering 15,000 customers across 85 countries, the Mews platform spans PMS, POS, RMS, Housekeeping and Payments, helping hoteliers move from property management to profit management. This new funding follows a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%. In 2025, the company achieved: 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk1 Platform Transaction Volumes of €16.7 billion ($19.7 billion) Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% Created more than €457 million ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in AI, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed – enabling properties to: Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation Optimise revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior Scale business without the debt of legacy tech Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale ,” said Kirk Lepke , Partner at EQT Growth. “ Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews .” Mews will also accelerate the expansion of Mews Payments and its broader FinTech infrastructure, embedding commerce into the core of hotel operations and positioning Mews as the connector between experience and revenue. The investment comes shortly after the completion of Mews’ fourteenth acquisition, DataChat, a generative AI analytics platform. Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ Matt and Richard have built a category-defining platform with the depth, pace of innovation and global reach required by modern hospitality ,” said Laura Connell , Partner at Atomico. “ The most ambitious builders in hospitality are focused on delivering ever-improving experiences for their end consumers, and they need technology that can keep pace with rising expectations around speed, service and personalisation. Mews is the key enabler for the future of hospitality, and the team is well on their way to building a generational company .” Share Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Together, we are creating a foundation for the next generation of hoteliers to thrive, not just today, but in a rapidly evolving industry that demands forward-thinking, scalable solutions.” ... What are the use cases of Mews?Copy Link
1. Use Cases by Category
   Front‑Desk Automation: Streamlines check‑in, check‑out and housekeeping communication. 
   Self‑service kiosks reduce check‑in time by 5 minutes.
   Digital key issuance eliminates front‑desk bottlenecks.
   Automated housekeeping notifications cut call volume.
   Revenue Management & Upselling: Optimizes pricing and drives ancillary sales. 
   Dynamic pricing engine (Atomize) boosts RevPAR by 39%.
   Embedded upsell offers increase average RevPAG by 70%.
   Direct‑booking engine lifts bookings by 45%.
   Guest Experience Personalisation: Creates seamless, end‑to‑end journeys. 
   Mews Guest Journey platform unifies booking, check‑in, in‑stay services.
   Online check‑in/out and mobile payments cut guest wait times.
   Smart‑device integrations (e.g., Operto) enable contact‑free stays.
   Multi‑Property & Chain Management: Centralises operations across dozens of locations. 
   Single dashboard controls 12,500+ properties worldwide.
   Unified reporting provides real‑time KPI visibility.
   Marketplace of 800+ third‑party apps scales functionality.
   Integrations & Marketplace: Extends core PMS with specialist tools. 
   Open API enables instant connection to accounting, AI, and distribution systems.
   Marketplace offers plug‑and‑play modules for loyalty, marketing, and POS.
   Seamless payment processing automates 100% of transactions.
2. Scenarios and Narratives
   Rapid On‑boarding of a Boutique Aparthotel: The property replaces a legacy system with Mews, achieving a two‑week remote deployment and 100% online onboarding. 
   Scenario: The hotel’s small IT team uses Mews Implementation Manager’s guided rollout, enabling staff to learn the interface in days. Guests now self‑check‑in via mobile, saving five minutes per arrival, while 60% of payments are processed automatically, freeing up admin time.
   Chain‑wide Revenue Optimisation: A hotel group leverages the Atomize RMS to adjust rates in real time across 30 locations. 
   Scenario: By feeding market data into the cloud‑native engine, the chain sees a 39% uplift in RevPAR and a 70% increase in average RevPAG through targeted upsells, all visible in a single analytics dashboard.
   Seamless Guest Journey for a Hostels Network: Hosts integrate Mews with smart‑lock and IoT platforms. 
   Scenario: Guests book online, receive a digital key on their phone, and can request housekeeping via the virtual concierge. The integration reduces front‑desk calls and improves NPS, while the marketplace supplies a one‑click loyalty app.
3. Strategic Benefits
   Operational Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks cuts staff workload, shortens check‑in times by five minutes and eliminates manual billing errors.
   Revenue Growth: Direct‑booking engine, dynamic pricing, and automated upsells deliver double‑digit percentage lifts in bookings and RevPAR.
   Scalability & Agility: Cloud‑native architecture supports rapid rollout to new properties without downtime, enabling multi‑property expansion.
   Guest Loyalty & Satisfaction: Unified guest journey and contact‑free services increase satisfaction scores and repeat bookings.
4. Core Functionalities
   Property Management System (PMS): Centralised reservation, billing, and housekeeping module with real‑time status updates.
   Revenue Management System (RMS – Atomize): AI‑driven pricing optimisation that reacts to market demand instantly.
   Booking Engine & Digital Key: Integrated front‑end for direct bookings and mobile key issuance.
   Mews Marketplace & Open API: Over 800 pre‑built integrations and a developer‑friendly API for custom extensions.
   Embedded Payments: PCI‑DSS‑compliant payment processing that automates 100% of transactions.
5. Customer Success Examples
   Locale (US aparthotel brand): Achieved 100% online onboarding, saved five minutes per check‑in, and drove >50% direct bookings with Mews Payments automating 60% of payments.
   HotelTechReport ranking: Named #1 Property Management System provider in the 2026 HotelTech Awards, with 93% of 773 surveyed hotels recommending Mews.
   Global hotel chain (12,500+ properties): Reported 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% RevPAR uplift, and 70% average RevPAG growth after switching to Mews.
6. Summary Table
| Category | Examples | Scenarios |
 --- 
| Front‑Desk Automation | Self‑service kiosks, digital keys, automated housekeeping alerts | Boutique aparthotel reduces check‑in time by 5 min, onboarding in 2 weeks |
| Revenue Management | Atomize RMS, upsell engine, direct‑booking portal | Chain sees +39 % RevPAR, +70 % RevPAG, +45 % direct bookings |
| Guest Experience | Online check‑in/out, mobile payments, guest journey platform | Hostel network offers contact‑free stays, reduces front‑desk calls |
| Multi‑Property Management | Single dashboard for 12,500+ properties, real‑time KPI reporting | Global brand scales operations without downtime |
| Integrations & Marketplace | 800+ third‑party apps, Open API, embedded payments | Hotels customise tech stack, automate 100 % of payments |
Sources:
   The Hospitality Management Software of the Future | Mews
   Locale customer story | Mews case study

HomeFundingWith €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand out across AI platforms
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
October 16, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower).
“_AI assistants are becoming the front door to the internet_,” says Daniel Espejo, CEO and Co-founder of Omnia. “_Omnia gives teams the playbook and the agents to act on it. Moving from dashboards to done. Just like Cursor makes engineers 10× more productive, Omnia makes marketers 10× more effective on the channels that matter now_.”
Omnia’s pre-Seed round aligns with a growing wave of European investment into agentic and AI-driven marketing platforms in 2025.
Comparable early-stage rounds include Massive Dynamic (€3 million, Paris) and Tandem (€3.2 million, Paris), both developing AI-based orchestration tools. Further up the scale, Sweden’s EvoluteIQ secured €44 million to expand its enterprise agentic-AI suite, while Italy’s SylloTips (€4.2 million) and the Netherlands’ Aizy (€1.5 million) also closed funding to advance AI-based marketing and agent platforms.
Within this context, there haven’t been many comparable funding rounds in Spain, positioning Omnia as an early mover in Spain’s emerging agentic-AI marketing ecosystem.
“_As search shifts rapidly from browsers to AI engines and assistants, every brand will need an agentic strategy to stay visible,_” saidRobert Jaeckle, Partner at Visionaries Club. “_Omnia is pioneering this space by building the agentic platform that turns visibility into action for marketeers. We’re so excited to partner with Daniel and Miguel as they define this new category for Europe and beyond._”
Omnia was founded in 2025 by Daniel Espejo (CEO), formerly at Klarna where he launched markets and scaled products used by millions, and Miguel Fernández (CTO), a platform engineer with experience at GitHub and Fastly.
Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.
Since launch, Omnia has been adopted by brands including Personio, ElevenLabs, Embat, Línea Directa, Ironhack, Air Europa, Praktika.ai , Trade Republic, Pleo and HappyRobot. ​
Also by marketing agencies like MIO group, Elogia, Product Hackers and Everybody. Early customers are allegedly reporting up to 3× visibility gains across AI channels.
“_Omnia has been key to our growth – helping us understand what potential clients are searching for, how well we’re positioned, and how to capture leads from AI across different markets,_” shared Adrian Varez, Head of Growth at Happy Robot.
The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.

HomeFundingMadrid-based startup Shakers raises €14 million to help businesses integrate teams of...
FundingSpain-Startups
Madrid-based startup Shakers raises €14 million to help businesses integrate teams of specialised talent with AI
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
HRTech startup Shakers, a Spanish workforce management platform connecting companies with tech freelancers, today announced its €14 million Series A funding round in order to expand double its headcount.
The funding round was led by Partech, with further investment from KFund, Brighteye Ventures, Athos Capital and Wayra.
Héctor Mata, Shakers CEO, said: “The convergence of AI and independent talent is not a trend – it’s a new operating model. Demand for specialised technical talent is soaring, but the traditional outsourcing model hasn’t evolved in over decades. With Shakers, we are setting a new standard for hiring: our platform acts as the new operating system for performance-driven freelancers — giving them the same benefits as traditional roles, while offering companies the agility to innovate faster. As we expand across Europe, we’re building the infrastructure layer that powers the AI-infused workplace of the future.”
Founded in 2021, Shakers is a workforce orchestration platform that connects businesses with specialised tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents. Founded by four serial entrepreneurs – Héctor Mata (CEO), Nico de Luis (COO), Adrián de Pedro (CPO) and Jaime Castillo (CRO) – with backgrounds in AI and software development, and first-hand experience in both finding freelance work and hiring talent.
With over 10,000 vetted freelancers and 450+ client companies including Microsoft, Inditex and Telefónica, Shakers aims to empower a new generation of performance-driven freeworkers with integrated services such as banking, insurance and productivity tools – all within its all-in-one Freelancer OS.
The company has grown over 6x in revenue over the past year, and is aiming to continue on its growth trajectory. 60% of their revenue reportedly comes from recurring customers and 30% of revenue generated organically from other European markets.
Shakers envisions a future of work where businesses can easily onboard specialised teams of tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents to collaborate, experiment, and drive new technological capabilities via complex and long-term projects. The platform empowers businesses and freelancers to automate their most “frustrating” workflow challenges and upgrade their productivity skills, promoting quick and effective results.
According to data provided by Shakers, this comes at a time when the traditional IT outsourcing landscape is being reshaped, with the European market projected to grow from €180 billion to over €280 billion by 2030 as demand shifts toward more flexible, high-skill talent solutions.
Shakers plans to double its team size in the next year to facilitate their growth, while expanding its AI agent integrations for freelancers and businesses. By 2027, Shakers will reportedly have orchestrated over 10,000 hybrid projects.
Simone Riva, Partech Senior Principal, said: “At Partech, we believe that while AI is reshaping the way we work, exceptional talent will always be at the heart of the future of work. From our very first conversation, it was clear the Shakers team is building something special — a fast, intuitive platform tailored to the flexible workforce of tomorrow. They’re redefining how companies find, hire, and collaborate with top talent. We’re proud to back this visionary team and are excited to support them as they scale across Spain, Europe, and beyond.”
As part of Shakers’ community of 10,000+ tech freelancers, they will enjoy the same benefits as traditional employment, including embedded services for banking, insurance, productivity and more, alongside the flexibility, freedom and incentives of self-employment.
According to Shakers, their technology goes beyond just sourcing – it validates talent end-to-end, assessing both hard and soft skills, experience, and cultural fit through more than 1,000 data points.
Integrated with Shakers Verified, their pre-vetting tool, the platform eliminates the need for early interviews and technical tests, delivering ready-to-work talent from day one. This means that companies can potentially go from idea to fully assembled team in minutes.

Executive Summary
Value Proposition & Problem: Mews offers a cloud‑native property management system that eliminates manual, fragmented hotel operations, enabling hoteliers to focus on guest experience and profitability.
Product / Service: A unified hospitality platform covering PMS, payments, revenue management, bookings, and marketplace integrations, with AI‑driven automation and an open API.
Market Stage & Traction: Over 12,500 properties in 85 countries use Mews, representing a customer base growth of 85% year‑over‑year and a transaction volume of $19.7 billion in 2025.
Achievements: Recognized as Best PMS (2024‑2026) and Best POS (2026) by Hotel Tech Report, 45% increase in direct bookings, 39% rise in RevPAR, and 70% average RevPAG uplift for customers.

Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World's Leading Hospitality Operating System
Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Published January 22, 2026 Press EQT Growth leads Series D funding round to accelerate the AI-native future of hotels globally, with participation from new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Investment values Mews at $2.5 billion Mews’ Platform Transaction Volume reached $19.7 billion in 2025 Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: [New York, 22 January 2026] – Mews , the operating system for hospitality, today announced it has raised $300 million in a Series D funding round led by EQT Growth , with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners , and participation from existing investors Kinnevik , Battery Ventures and Tiger Global . This investment marks a defining moment for hospitality technology and values the company at $2.5 billion. Following a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%, this fundraise establishes Mews as the leading hospitality tech provider for hotels of all sizes. In 2025, the company achieved: Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: - A customer base of 15,000, with 132,000+ monthly active hoteliers in 85 countries - 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk 1 - Platform Transaction Volumes of $19.7 billion - Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% - Created more than half a billion ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces 2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations “With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry,” said Matt Welle, CEO of Mews. “We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in artificial intelligence, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed - enabling properties to: - Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation - Optimize revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior - Scale business without the debt of legacy tech - Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “Hospitality is the business of experiences,” said Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry.” A new standard for a global industry Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: “I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale,” said Kirk Lepke, Partner at EQT Growth. “Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews.” Section Title: Mews Secures $300 million Investment to Cement Position as World’s Leading Hospitality Operating ... Content: Mews will also accelerate the expansion of Mews Payments and its broader fintech infrastructure, embedding commerce into the core of hotel operations and positioning Mews as the connector between experience and revenue. In addition, the company will continue its ambitious international expansion across its primary geographies of North America and Europe, as well as expanding into new markets. ... Funding Netherland-Startups Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content:  By David Cendon Garcia January 22, 2026 Share Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Facebook Twitter WhatsApp Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Linkedin Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content:  Amsterdam-based Mews , an OS for hospitality, today announced it has raised €255 million ($300 million) in a Series D funding round in order to further invest in AI, embed agent-driven systems to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, improve the guest experience, and accelerate how products are built and deployed The round was led by EQT Growth, with new investors Atomico and HarbourVest Partners, and participation from existing investors Kinnevik, Battery Ventures and Tiger Global – valuing the company at €2.1 billion ($2.5 billion). Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ With EQT Growth joining in addition to new investors Atomico and HarbourVest, we have the backing to continue moving faster than anyone else in the industry, ” says Matt Welle , CEO of Mews. “ We are engineering an operating system that is changing how hoteliers interact with their guests. Mews exists to handle the operational complexity so hoteliers can focus on what matters: making hospitality even more fun, profitable, and fulfilling .” In the context of recent European hospitality and HotelTech funding activity, Amsterdam-based Mews’ €255 million Series D represents one of the largest pure-play software raises in the sector. ... Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Taken together, these announcements indicate that roughly €695 million has been committed to European hospitality and adjacent HotelTech models in 2025/2026, with Mews’ raise accounting for a substantial share of that total. Also of note, is that in March 2024 Mews also raised €101 million to help hoteliers embrace cloud technology . “ Hospitality is the business of experiences, ” adds Richard Valtr, Founder of Mews. “ The validation for our product from the market is clear, in both the US and Europe, and it is great to see how we are now powering ahead of any other hospitality company in terms of AI and agentic hospitality. It’s an exciting time to reinforce our vision of making Mews hotels the most profitable in the industry. ” Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: Founded in 2012, Mews an OS for hospitality, unifying workflows across revenue, operations and the guest journey so teams can automate the mundane and focus on memorable guest experiences. Powering 15,000 customers across 85 countries, the Mews platform spans PMS, POS, RMS, Housekeeping and Payments, helping hoteliers move from property management to profit management. This new funding follows a year of strong and durable growth, during which SaaS gross profit increased by 55%. In 2025, the company achieved: 42.3 million checked-in reservations, of which 3.2 million via Mews Kiosk1 Platform Transaction Volumes of €16.7 billion ($19.7 billion) Accelerated SaaS gross profit growth of 55% Created more than €457 million ($537 million) in additional revenue for hoteliers through its proprietary Mews Spaces2 feature, powered by over two million non-room reservations Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: The funding will expand Mews’ investments in AI, embedding agent-driven systems across the platform to automate complex workflows, reduce cognitive load for staff, significantly improve the guest experience and accelerate how products are built and deployed – enabling properties to: Orchestrate operations in real time with intelligent automation Optimise revenue and profit with advanced insights into guest behavior Scale business without the debt of legacy tech Radically simplify workflows to onboard teams faster Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ I’ve had the pleasure of getting to know the Mews team for several years and witness the company go from a bold vision to an organization delivering at scale ,” said Kirk Lepke , Partner at EQT Growth. “ Hospitality is one of the world’s largest industries, yet its core systems remain decades behind. Mews is creating a modern technology standard, an AI-enabled hospitality operating system that helps solve the fragmentation we see in the industry. We at EQT Growth are incredibly excited to lead this round and partner with Mews .” Mews will also accelerate the expansion of Mews Payments and its broader FinTech infrastructure, embedding commerce into the core of hotel operations and positioning Mews as the connector between experience and revenue. The investment comes shortly after the completion of Mews’ fourteenth acquisition, DataChat, a generative AI analytics platform. Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi... Content: “ Matt and Richard have built a category-defining platform with the depth, pace of innovation and global reach required by modern hospitality ,” said Laura Connell , Partner at Atomico. “ The most ambitious builders in hospitality are focused on delivering ever-improving experiences for their end consumers, and they need technology that can keep pace with rising expectations around speed, service and personalisation. Mews is the key enabler for the future of hospitality, and the team is well on their way to building a generational company .” Share Section Title: With €255 million in funding, Dutch company Mews doubles down on automation and payments in hospi...
