Home » Funding news
Anyformat Secures €3.3M to Scale Document Intelligence

By Ekaterina Pisareva
November 27, 2025
Anyformat team
Credits: Anyformat official website
Spanish AI startup Anyformat has closed a €3.3 million seed round to accelerate the development of its document intelligence platform. The funding was led by Kibo Ventures, with continued support from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which participated in the company's previous round.
The Madrid-based company has built a generative AI platform that extracts and structures complex data from virtually any document type. Its technology enables large organizations to process contracts, invoices, emails, and presentations with what the company describes as human-level accuracy at machine speed, while keeping sensitive data under full organizational control.
A New Approach to Document Intelligence
What sets Anyformat apart is its ability to combine extracted document information with a company's existing proprietary data in real time. This means businesses can automate the analysis of unstructured documents while simultaneously cross-referencing them against internal records like customer databases, policies, product catalogs, or historical information.
For organizations juggling complex documentation across fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validation steps, reduces errors, and speeds up business-critical processes. The opportunity is significant: according to Dell Technologies' Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to turn their data into valuable, real-time insights.
"The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that enables companies to automate critical processes while preserving data sovereignty, an essential requirement in Europe. The founders' vision and talent, combined with the vast potential of AI in a large and growing market, were key factors in our decision to invest," says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner at Kibo Ventures.
Expanding Capabilities and European Reach
The fresh capital will fuel product development and grow the company's engineering and product teams. Priorities include strengthening advanced extraction capabilities and building proprietary error-detection models. Anyformat also plans to establish a dedicated sales team focused on Spain and the broader European Union market.
The company will continue investing in its privacy-first infrastructure and is working toward ISO 27001 certification, a requirement for many large corporations and public institutions.
Anyformat forecasts surpassing €1 million in annual recurring revenue in 2026, driven by European expansion and growing demand for automation, compliance tools, and advanced document processing solutions.
From Startup to Enterprise Partner
Founded by CEO Juan Huguet and CTO Diego Pérez, Anyformat has already secured projects with the Singapore Government, L'Oréal Group, and IAG Group. These partnerships demonstrate the platform's ability to operate in complex, high-demand environments.
"Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to help companies analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the level of security required by modern regulations," says Huguet. Pérez adds: "We are building technology that goes far beyond simple document digitization."
Since raising €520,000 in June 2025, the company has significantly expanded its capabilities beyond traditional document extraction. It now positions itself as the only provider capable of real-time integration between extracted document data and enterprise systems.
Investors Back Long-Term Vision
The seed round brings together a strong syndicate of Spanish and European investors. Kibo Ventures led the round, while 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures all doubled down on their earlier investments. The continued backing from all previous investors signals confidence in Anyformat's trajectory and market positioning.
The company continues refining its technology to convert any file into structured data for analytics, business intelligence, and operational workflows, always maintaining strict privacy and security standards.
More about:  AI | Europe | Funding | Generative AI | Seed Funding | Spain | Startups | Venture capital
Spanish AI startup Anyformat has closed a €3.3 million seed round to accelerate the development of its document intelligence platform. The funding was led by Kibo Ventures, with continued support from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which participated in the company's previous round.
The Madrid-based company has built a generative AI platform that extracts and structures complex data from virtually any document type. Its technology enables large organizations to process contracts, invoices, emails, and presentations with what the company describes as human-level accuracy at machine speed, while keeping sensitive data under full organizational control.
A New Approach to Document Intelligence
What sets Anyformat apart is its ability to combine extracted document information with a company's existing proprietary data in real time. This means businesses can automate the analysis of unstructured documents while simultaneously cross-referencing them against internal records like customer databases, policies, product catalogs, or historical information.
For organizations juggling complex documentation across fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validation steps, reduces errors, and speeds up business-critical processes. The opportunity is significant: according to Dell Technologies' Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to turn their data into valuable, real-time insights.
"The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that enables companies to automate critical processes while preserving data sovereignty, an essential requirement in Europe. The founders' vision and talent, combined with the vast potential of AI in a large and growing market, were key factors in our decision to invest," says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner at Kibo Ventures.
Expanding Capabilities and European Reach
The fresh capital will fuel product development and grow the company's engineering and product teams. Priorities include strengthening advanced extraction capabilities and building proprietary error-detection models. Anyformat also plans to establish a dedicated sales team focused on Spain and the broader European Union market.
The company will continue investing in its privacy-first infrastructure and is working toward ISO 27001 certification, a requirement for many large corporations and public institutions.
Anyformat forecasts surpassing €1 million in annual recurring revenue in 2026, driven by European expansion and growing demand for automation, compliance tools, and advanced document processing solutions.

Kibo Ventures led the round, while 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures all doubled down on their earlier investments. Anyformat closes a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures
anyformat_team
01/12/2025  |  sradmin
Anyformat,  a generative AI platform specialized in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures, with follow-on participation from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which also supported the company’s previous round.
The company is building the next generation of document infrastructure powered by generative AI. Its platform enables operational teams in large organizations to process complex documents with human-level accuracy at machine speed, while retaining full control over sensitive data. The technology is designed for seamless deployment from day one, requiring minimal technical dependencies.
Since its previous €520,000 pre-seed round, Anyformat has expanded its capabilities well beyond traditional extraction. It is now the only provider that can combine extracted document information with internal, proprietary company data in real time. This means that contracts, invoices, or emails can be automatically analyzed while being cross-referenced with corporate databases such as customer records, policies, product catalogs, or historical data.
For organizations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors, and accelerates critical business processes. This represents a real problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies fail to convert their data into valuable information in real time, highlighting the urgency for solutions such as Anyformat.
“”The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that allows companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key issue in Europe. The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai, a company also invested in by Kibo Ventures, combined with the possibilities offered by AI in a huge market, were key factors in our decision to invest,” says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner of Kibo Ventures.,” says Javier Torremocha, partner at Kibo Ventures.
With a team of nine full-time employees, the startup continues to refine its technology to convert any document, presentation, or file into data ready for analysis, business intelligence, or internal operations, always with the strictest privacy controls.
Growth underway: €1 million ARR forecast for 2026
Anyformat aims to exceed €1 million in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion in Europe and the increase in use cases linked to automation, compliance, and advanced document processing.
Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat, says “Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to enable companies to analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the security required by regulations. We are building the new European document infrastructure.” Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO of the company, adds, “We are developing technology capable of going far beyond document digitization.”
The investment: technology, expansion, and compliance
This new funding will enable the company to accelerate its product development and strengthen areas critical to its growth. A significant portion of the funds will be used to expand the engineering and product team, driving its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error detection models. At the same time, Anyformat will build a highly focused sales team in Spain and the European Union to capitalize on the growing demand for intelligent data processing solutions.
In addition, the company will continue to invest in its privacy-first infrastructure, strengthening security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001, which is key to working with large corporations and public administrations.
With this round of funding, Anyformat is accelerating its path to becoming the European benchmark in document AI. The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organizations that cannot compromise on accuracy or data control. In a market moving towards more autonomous, private, and multimodal solutions, Anyformat is setting the course for how generative AI will be applied in document management over the next decade.

Madrid-based Anyformat raises €520k to simplify the automation of unstructured data extraction
Stefano De Marzo

Anyformat, a Spanish startup betting on a new era of collaboration between people and artificial intelligence, has announced the raising of €520k in a pre-seed financing round. This round, led by 4Founders Capital, Abac Nest Ventures, and prominent business angels, will allow anyformat to accelerate the development of its generative artificial intelligence platform, designed to transform the way companies manage and analyze unstructured data.
With this injection of capital, anyformat is consolidating itself as a key solution to automate the extraction and analysis of complex data, enabling any user – without the need for technical knowledge – to convert large volumes of unstructured information into strategic insights. This marks a crucial step for organizations looking to optimize processes and improve data-driven decision making.
According to the Data Cube report “Spain Artificial Intelligence Market Growth and Performance”, the artificial intelligence market in Spain is expected to reach $26.56 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 27.4%. This growth environment, combined with the exponential increase in the generation of unstructured data, reinforces the strategic relevance of anyformat for companies seeking operational agility and efficiency.
Juan Huguet, CEO of anyformat, said: “Our mission is clear: to enable organizations to adopt hybrid intelligence by allowing AI to do the heavy lifting of extracting and structuring data, and getting people to focus on solving strategic and creative problems. With anyformat, companies access valuable information quickly and without complications.”
The anyformat platform automates the extraction of information contained in documents, presentations or even voice recordings, simplifying what previously required a lot of manual work or advanced technical knowledge. In the words of Alejandro Fernández, COO of anyformat, “Many companies are held back by the difficulty of managing unstructured data. With our solution, we make that information immediately available to teams, ready to be integrated into tools such as Excel or business intelligence platforms.”
In addition to its focus on usability, anyformat is designed to scale easily, ensuring simple integration with existing systems. Diego Pérez, CTO of anyformat, added: “Our priority is to optimize workflows without compromising data security or privacy. We know that trust is key, and we ensure that information is handled responsibly and efficiently, allowing organizations to grow sustainably.”
Investors recognize anyformat’s disruptive potential. Joan Pina, partner at Abac Nest Ventures, commented: “At Abac Nest Ventures we have bet on anyformat because we believe in the vision of its founders and in the impact that its technology can have on the industry. Turning unstructured data into actionable knowledge is a growing need, and companies using anyformat are gaining competitiveness by quickly and efficiently integrating AI into their processes.”
With the new funding, anyformat is preparing to expand its team and operations, with an eye toward consolidating its presence in Spain and exploring opportunities in international markets. “At anyformat, we are committed to transforming unstructured information into a competitive advantage, allowing companies to not only access valuable information, but to use it intelligently to make strategic decisions that drive their growth and success,” concluded Juan Huguet.
Stefano De Marzo

Anyformat Secures €3.3 Million to Revolutionize Document Management
Funding Round Overview
Recently, Anyformat has successfully closed a €3.3 million seed funding round, with Kibo Ventures leading the investment. This round also saw participation from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which have previously supported the company. This capital will be instrumental in enhancing its innovative document management solutions.
Transforming Document Management
Anyformat specializes in offering generative AI solutions that simplify how organizations handle their documents. Their platform is designed to extract and structure complex data seamlessly, turning traditional document management approaches on their heads. This technology significantly speeds up the processing of various documents such as contracts, invoices, and presentations, achieving human-level accuracy while retaining strict control over sensitive data.
Broader Market Applications

The company has moved beyond mere document extraction, offering capabilities that merge extracted information with a company's proprietary data instantly.
Real-Time Analysis and Benefits
This unique offer allows for the automated analysis of unstructured documents while cross-referencing vital data with internal resources, such as customer databases and historical records. This technological advancement is particularly advantageous for organizations grappling with complex documentation and disjointed systems, largely mitigating manual validation steps and reducing the probability of errors.
Addressing Industry Needs

Future Growth Projections

Investment in Technology
The fresh funding will significantly boost product development and allow Anyformat to enhance its engineering and product teams, contributing to the advancement of its extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error-detection models. Moreover, a dedicated sales team focused on Spain and the European Union will be established to drive growth.
Commitment to Privacy Standards
Anyformat remains focused on maintaining a privacy-first infrastructure and aims to achieve relevant certifications such as ISO 27001, which is crucial for serving large organizations and public institutions. This commitment to data protection not only further solidifies its market position but also reassures clients about the handling of their sensitive information.

supporting_evidence.title: Why we invested in Anyformat
supporting_evidence.snippet:
AnyFormat’s Solution: A GenAI Platform for Unstructured Data
With advanced GenAI capabilities, AnyFormat transforms unstructured data into structured, actionable insights, enabling businesses to make faster, smarter decisions. Built on a foundation of advanced AI models, the platform can handle a variety of data formats and integrates seamlessly with enterprise systems, prioritizing data privacy and cost efficiency. [...] The Platform: AnyFormat’s tool enables users to configure data extractions easily. Users can create, review, or delete extraction configurations, download extracted data, or publish an API to process files programmatically. The platform effortlessly scales from single files to millions, with AnyFormat managing the complexity of a full GenAI-powered extraction pipeline in the background. [...] AnyFormat’s founding team brings exceptional expertise to the GenAI landscape. CEO Juan Huguet-García holds a Ph.D. in Nuclear Physics and has years of hands-on experience developing and implementing GenAI solutions. Alongside co-founders Alejandro Fernández and Diego Pérez-Sastre, who each bring deep technical skills from their work in Clarity AI’s GenAI division, the team has a firsthand understanding of the challenges around unstructured data processing. Together, they designed AnyFormat to address the data quality, privacy, and usability gaps they encountered in previous roles. This highly qualified team is well-equipped to push the boundaries of what’s possible in data automation.  You can hear more about Juan’s perspective on unstructured data in his recent Itnig Podcast interview.
supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Joan Pina Ollé
Why we invested in Anyformat
Joan Pina Ollé
Nov 05, 2024
At Abac Nest Ventures, we support technologies that drive meaningful change in business operations. AnyFormat, a Madrid-based GenAI platform, redefines how companies approach unstructured data. From PDFs and images to audio files, unstructured data is a potential goldmine for insights—but extracting these efficiently has long been a challenge. We’re excited to join AnyFormat’s €520k pre-seed round to support their journey in solving this critical problem for businesses. 
The Challenge: 80% of Business Data Is Untapped
In today’s data-driven economy, approximately 80% of a company’s information assets are unstructured, often buried inside documents like spreadsheets, PDFs, and files used alongside ERP systems and other technologies. Despite advancements, businesses still rely on these side documents, making capturing and leveraging all their data challenging. This unstructured data holds valuable insights for decision-making across finance, healthcare, and insurance industries. Yet traditional extraction methods are often too costly, inefficient, and complex, leaving much of this data untapped. AnyFormat bridges this gap by automating the extraction and structuring of unstructured data, making it accessible, actionable, and cost-effective. 
AnyFormat’s Solution: A GenAI Platform for Unstructured Data
With advanced GenAI capabilities, AnyFormat transforms unstructured data into structured, actionable insights, enabling businesses to make faster, smarter decisions. Built on a foundation of advanced AI models, the platform can handle a variety of data formats and integrates seamlessly with enterprise systems, prioritizing data privacy and cost efficiency. 
 The Platform: AnyFormat’s tool enables users to configure data extractions easily. Users can create, review, or delete extraction configurations, download extracted data, or publish an API to process files programmatically. The platform effortlessly scales from single files to millions, with AnyFormat managing the complexity of a full GenAI-powered extraction pipeline in the background.
 The Processing Engine: AnyFormat’s core technology leverages foundational models such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and others, depending on the required skill sets. This "engine and bodywork" approach allows AnyFormat to pull from best-in-class models for optimal performance. On top of these foundational models, AnyFormat layers its proprietary models to fine-tune and train the data extraction pipeline with precision and efficiency. The engine is organized into five pillars:
+ Integrations: Flexible data input via APIs, cloud storage, and more.
+ Data Transformations: Standardizes complex data using a combination of classical OCR algorithms, vision models, content classifiers, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
+ Data Extractions: Fine-tuned Small Language Models (SLMs) ensure high-accuracy extraction which can be tailored to each client’s needs.
+ Data Validations: AI-driven validation checks data quality, including schema validation and reliability scores.
+ Data Exports: Delivers structured data in formats ready for integration, such as CSV, JSON, and SQL.
Each pillar is designed to handle diverse data types and sources, transform them as required, accurately extract relevant information, validate data integrity, and export it in the desired format. This flexible architecture makes AnyFormat a powerful comprehensive, scalable data processing tool. AnyFormat’s lightweight, secure SLMs also support local data processing, which reduces costs and enhances privacy.
The Founders: Firsthand AI Experience with a Vision for Change
AnyFormat’s founding team brings exceptional expertise to the GenAI landscape. CEO Juan Huguet-García holds a Ph.D. in Nuclear Physics and has years of hands-on experience developing and implementing GenAI solutions. Alongside co-founders Alejandro Fernández and Diego Pérez-Sastre, who each bring deep technical skills from their work in Clarity AI’s GenAI division, the team has a firsthand understanding of the challenges around unstructured data processing. Together, they designed AnyFormat to address the data quality, privacy, and usability gaps they encountered in previous roles. This highly qualified team is well-equipped to push the boundaries of what’s possible in data automation.  You can hear more about Juan’s perspective on unstructured data in his recent Itnig Podcast interview. 
A Market Primed for Transformation
The Intelligent Document Processing (IDP) market is projected to reach $20 billion by 2032, growing at a 30% CAGR. This shift underscores the importance of data automation for scaling operations and improving efficiency. AnyFormat has already launched pilot programs with clients in high-demand sectors like insurance and travel, proving its ability to streamline workflows and enhance decision-making. With a growing market demand and a scalable, efficient solution, AnyFormat is well-positioned for significant impact.
Our Decision: Investing in the Future of Data Automation
The vast amount of unstructured data generated across industries has created a pressing need for tools to convert raw information into valuable insights. GenAI now offers the power to transform this data into actionable knowledge, paving the way for innovation and competitive advantages across sectors. We believe AnyFormat is the team positioned to lead this transformation. With a flexible, efficient platform and deep expertise in AI, AnyFormat enables companies to unlock the value of unstructured data. This vision of data automation as a driver of industry-wide impact is why we proudly support AnyFormat in their €520k pre-seed round. 
> For more insights into AnyFormat’s mission and technology, see recent features in EU-Startups and Yahoo Finance or visit their website at anyformat.ai. 
> Follow Juan Huguet, Anyformat’s CEO on LinkedIn, where he regularly discusses recent developments in GenAI.
Thanks for reading Abac Nest Ventures! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

supporting_evidence.raw_content:
|  |  |  |
 --- 
|  |  |  |

Madrid’s Anyformat closes €3.3 million round to scale its agentic OCR and data-sovereignty-focused document tools
EU Startupsby EU Startups

Anyformat, a Spanish GenAI platform specialising in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a Seed round of €3.3 million to expand the engineering and product team, driving its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error detection models.
The round was led by Kibo Ventures and followed by 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures and Decelera Ventures, funds that already participated in the previous round.
Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat, says, “Documents are the backbone of any organisation. Our goal is to enable companies to analyse them with human intelligence, machine speed and the security required by regulation. We are building the new European document infrastructure.‘ (Translated).
Among European startups working on document processing, data extraction and AI-enabled enterprise automation, 2025 funding activity has been limited but illustrative: France-based Klaimy raised €1.2 million to advance its AI assistant for processing medical documentation, while Prague-headquartered Resistant AI secured €21 million in Series B financing to strengthen its document-fraud detection and financial-crime prevention systems.
Together, these comparable 2025 rounds amount to roughly €22.2 million, indicating that investor attention in this domain remains focused on specialised applications such as insurance, medical underwriting and fraud detection rather than broad, general-purpose document-infrastructure platforms.
In this context, Anyformat’s €3.3 million Seed roundpositions it as one of the few European companies pursuing a horizontal, generative-AI-based document-intelligence layer – particularly notable given the absence of other Spain-based fundraises in the category – adding meaningful weight to the still-emerging European document-automation landscape.
Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO of the company, adds: “We are developing technology that goes far beyond document digitisation.” (Translated)
Founded in 2024, Anyformat is innovating a new generation of document infrastructure based on generative AI, which reportedly allows the operational teams of large corporations to work with complex documents with human precision and machine speed, while maintaining control of the data at all times.
Since its previous round, where it raised €520k in pre-Seed funding (as covered by EU-Startups), Anyformat has expanded its offering beyond classic extraction. Today, the company says they are the only provider that allows the information extracted from a document to be combined in real time with internal and proprietary company data.
This means that a contract, invoice or email can be analysed automatically while being cross-referenced directly with corporate databases, such as customers, policies, catalogues or history.
For organisations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors and accelerates critical business processes.
This represents a real problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to convert their data into valuable information in real time.
“The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that allows companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key issue in Europe.
“The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai, a company also invested in by Kibo Ventures, combined with the possibilities offered by AI in a huge market, were key factors in our decision to invest,” shares Javier Torremocha, co-founder and Managing Partner of Kibo Ventures.
Anyformat aims to exceed €1 million in ARR in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion in Europe and the increase in use cases linked to automation, compliance and advanced document processing.
In addition, the company will continue to invest in its privacy-first infrastructure, strengthening security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001.
The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organisations that cannot compromise on accuracy or data control.

Abac Nest II FCRE (Abac Nest II) develops the VC investment strategy of Abac Capital. Abac Nest II is a fund managed by Abac Capital Manager, SGEIC, S.A., and features a team that combines extensive experience as Entrepreneurs, Investors, and Operating Partners
We invest in Founders with conviction and purpose. We offer extensive support both in investment and resources to achieve a purpose with which we are aligned.
At Abac Nest we invest from €50k to €500k per deal in Founders with the ability to create large-scale technology-based businesses.
In our investment process, we are clear, direct and agile. We apply the rigor necessary to identify and support exciting business opportunities.

Tickets
We invest between €50k and €500k per deal in technology startups that can create highly scalable businesses.

Borja Martinez de la Rosa  
Founding Partner
Borja co-founded Abac Capital in 2014. Before co-founding Abac Capital, he worked at Apax Partners for 13 years as a Partner in the tech & telecom team globally and as a generalist covering the Spanish market since 2004. Prior to joining Apax Partners, he was a Summer Associate at Goldman Sachs and an Associate at The Boston Consulting Group. Borja holds a degree in Business Administration from ESADE (Barcelona) and an MBA from Harvard Business School (Boston), where he was a Fulbright Scholar.

Joan Pina  
Partner
Joan Pina is a Serial Entrepreneur who has been part of Abac Capital since 2017. Together with Borja and Rubén Bonet, Joan is one of the promoters of Abac Nest. Prior to Abac Nest, Joan founded four digital companies, including a fintech and insurtech company, and was one of the leading shareholders in LetsBonus (acquired by an Amazon subsidiary: Living Social) where he held the position of CEO . Previously, he developed his professional career at PwC in New York, Dublin and Barcelona. Joan has a degree in Business Administration and Management from the University of Barcelona and participated in a PwC Executive Development program at the IESE Business School. Joan is a fan of long distance Triathlons and passionate about learning about new business models.

Rubén Bonet  
Venture Partner
Rubén has been one of the driving forces behind Abac Nest since its inception. Rubén is the co-founder, Chairman and CEO of Fractus since 1999, one of the most successful deep tech companies in Europe, specialized in global wireless and telecom industries, named a Technology Pioneer in 2005 by the World Economic Forum. Since 2016. He is also a Chairman and Board Member of IP Europe, representing European SMEs. Rubén has extensive experience leading a start-up towards scale-up, with high-level international fundraising and in particular in start-ups with a high technological component due to his experience and network in Deep Tech. Author of the book on entrepreneurship ‘Fractus , the phoenix’, Rubén is a fan of Endurance sports, having completed a Marathon des Sables.

Marta Campillo  
Principal

Tristán Conde

Additionally, he attended specialized programs in Paris, France and Boston, United States. After interning at Abac Capital, he joined the Abac Nest team to further delve into his passion for startup innovation.

María Mas

Prior to that she interned at an engineering company and in a non-profit organisation in Peru, CCAJO Asociación Jesús Obrero. In February 2021, Abac certified as a B Corporation. B Corps are organisations that meet the highest verified standards of social and environmental performance, transparency, and accountability. supporting_evidence.snippet:
Juan Huguet, CEO & Co-founder of Anyformat at #DeceleraMenorca2025
The Challenge: Unstructured Data Is Everywhere
Modern organizations spend millions trying to extract and standardize data buried in scattered formats. Legacy systems break down, manual processes are slow and error-prone, and scalability remains a dream.
AnyFormat changes the game. With seamless integrations, enterprise-grade security, and rapid workflow configuration, it enables teams to extract, validate, and export structured data at scale, without writing a single line of code.
Why We Invested in AnyFormat [...] Saltar al contenido 
Decelera
Contact
Decelera
Contact
Why We Invested in AnyFormat – Turning Data Chaos into Clarity
Escrito por Marina Bestué
At Decelera Ventures, we back founders who tackle deep, structural inefficiencies with bold vision and elegant execution. AnyFormat does exactly that, turning the overwhelming chaos of unstructured data into clean, actionable insight.
From insurance to banking, companies today are drowning in PDFs, images, emails, and audio files filled with valuable information, but trapped in formats machines can’t understand. AnyFormat unlocks that data with a GenAI platform that delivers over 99% accuracy, reduces costs by more than 90%, and requires zero technical expertise to implement. [...] Diego Pérez, Juan Huguet & Alejandro Fernández, founders of Anyformat 2024
Building the Future of Data
We believe AnyFormat is building foundational infrastructure for the GenAI era. In a world where data complexity grows exponentially, they are offering clarity, speed, and control. They aren’t just transforming how businesses extract data, they’re shaping how organizations make decisions.
At Decelera, we support founders who solve hard problems with lasting impact. That’s exactly what AnyFormat is doing. And we’re proud to back them.If you’re a founder looking to scale with purpose, apply for our next program and be part of a community that helps you grow beyond the grind.

Why We Invested in AnyFormat – Turning Data Chaos into Clarity
Escrito por Marina Bestué
At Decelera Ventures, we back founders who tackle deep, structural inefficiencies with bold vision and elegant execution. AnyFormat does exactly that, turning the overwhelming chaos of unstructured data into clean, actionable insight.
From insurance to banking, companies today are drowning in PDFs, images, emails, and audio files filled with valuable information, but trapped in formats machines can’t understand. AnyFormat unlocks that data with a GenAI platform that delivers over 99% accuracy, reduces costs by more than 90%, and requires zero technical expertise to implement.

Juan Huguet, CEO & Co-founder of Anyformat at #DeceleraMenorca2025
The Challenge: Unstructured Data Is Everywhere
Modern organizations spend millions trying to extract and standardize data buried in scattered formats. Legacy systems break down, manual processes are slow and error-prone, and scalability remains a dream.
AnyFormat changes the game. With seamless integrations, enterprise-grade security, and rapid workflow configuration, it enables teams to extract, validate, and export structured data at scale, without writing a single line of code.
Why We Invested in AnyFormat
At Decelera, we look for teams that combine technical mastery with strategic clarity. Juan, Diego, and Alejandro have worked at the frontier of AI and data infrastructure, most notably at Clarity AI, one of the fastest-scaling impact-tech companies in Europe. They know what it takes to build robust, enterprise-ready AI products.
But beyond their credentials, they have built a platform that solves a real pain point for modern businesses, and does so with precision, speed, and empathy for the end user.
Juan: The Scientist Who Sees the System
With a PhD in Physics and a deep background in machine learning, Juan Huguet doesn’t just lead, he architects intelligence. At IBM and Clarity AI, he led complex AI projects; now, as CEO of AnyFormat, he channels that experience into creating infrastructure that scales effortlessly while adapting to real-world use cases.
Diego: The Quiet Power Behind the Platform
As CTO, Diego Pérez builds what others call impossible. With a strong foundation in AI and MLOps, he ensures that AnyFormat’s core engine is both technically elegant and deeply reliable. Whether it’s designing robust model pipelines or architecting seamless scalability, Diego makes sure the engine runs with zero compromise.
Alejandro: The Strategic Operator
Alejandro Fernández brings structure and momentum. With experience across EY, PwC, and Clarity AI, he drives operations with the precision of an engineer and the insight of a strategist. At AnyFormat, he makes sure the solution doesn’t just work, but fits perfectly into clients’ most critical workflows.

Diego Pérez, Juan Huguet & Alejandro Fernández, founders of Anyformat 2024

Anyformat closes a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures
anyformat_team
01/12/2025  |  sradmin
Anyformat,  a generative AI platform specialized in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures, with follow-on participation from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which also supported the company’s previous round.
The company is building the next generation of document infrastructure powered by generative AI. Its platform enables operational teams in large organizations to process complex documents with human-level accuracy at machine speed, while retaining full control over sensitive data. The technology is designed for seamless deployment from day one, requiring minimal technical dependencies.
Since its previous €520,000 pre-seed round, Anyformat has expanded its capabilities well beyond traditional extraction. It is now the only provider that can combine extracted document information with internal, proprietary company data in real time. This means that contracts, invoices, or emails can be automatically analyzed while being cross-referenced with corporate databases such as customer records, policies, product catalogs, or historical data.
For organizations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors, and accelerates critical business processes. This represents a real problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies fail to convert their data into valuable information in real time, highlighting the urgency for solutions such as Anyformat.
“”The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that allows companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key issue in Europe. The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai, a company also invested in by Kibo Ventures, combined with the possibilities offered by AI in a huge market, were key factors in our decision to invest,” says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner of Kibo Ventures.,” says Javier Torremocha, partner at Kibo Ventures.
With a team of nine full-time employees, the startup continues to refine its technology to convert any document, presentation, or file into data ready for analysis, business intelligence, or internal operations, always with the strictest privacy controls.
Growth underway: €1 million ARR forecast for 2026
Anyformat aims to exceed €1 million in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion in Europe and the increase in use cases linked to automation, compliance, and advanced document processing.
Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat, says “Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to enable companies to analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the security required by regulations. We are building the new European document infrastructure.” Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO of the company, adds, “We are developing technology capable of going far beyond document digitization.”
The investment: technology, expansion, and compliance
This new funding will enable the company to accelerate its product development and strengthen areas critical to its growth. A significant portion of the funds will be used to expand the engineering and product team, driving its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error detection models. At the same time, Anyformat will build a highly focused sales team in Spain and the European Union to capitalize on the growing demand for intelligent data processing solutions.
In addition, the company will continue to invest in its privacy-first infrastructure, strengthening security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001, which is key to working with large corporations and public administrations.
With this round of funding, Anyformat is accelerating its path to becoming the European benchmark in document AI. The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organizations that cannot compromise on accuracy or data control. In a market moving towards more autonomous, private, and multimodal solutions, Anyformat is setting the course for how generative AI will be applied in document management over the next decade.

evidence.raw_content:
Logo

Logo

Logo
Czech it out: HRTech startup Talentiqa raises €1 million to let AI do the first job interview – even at midnight
David Cendon Garcia
Talentiqa
Talentiqa, a recruitment automation startup out of Prague, has secured €1 million in funding to expand its AI-powered hiring assistant.
The round was equally backed by Purple Ventures and Venture to Future Fund.
“For twenty years I dealt with hiring hundreds of people in retail, hospitality, and gastronomy, and we constantly ran into the same issue. Companies complained there weren’t enough candidates, while applicants complained that firms didn’t know how to communicate with them – often not replying at all. These two worlds were simply missing each other. Sometimes the problem was as trivial as an applicant not being able to answer the phone in time.
“We founded Talentiqa to speed up the initial interview and increase its success rate. Thanks to AI, we can now automate the most difficult part at scale – the first contact, verifying interest, and quickly connecting the right people with the right companies,” says Marek Dian, Co-founder and CEO of Talentiqa.
In 2025, European HRTech startups have continued to attract steady investor interest, with several funding rounds reflecting the sector’s shift toward AI-driven hiring and workforce management.
Danish startup Kiku secured €4 million to address high-volume recruitment, while London-based Metaview raised around €30 million to expand its AI recruiting platform. In Southern Europe, Orbio in Madrid closed €6.4 million to scale its AI-native HCM system, and Skillvue in Milan raised €5.5 million to support skills-based recruitment.
Against this backdrop, Talentiqa’s round represents a comparatively smaller raise but positions it within the same European trend of automating hiring workflows through AI.
While its peers are advancing skills platforms, HCM systems, or payroll solutions, Talentiqa seems to differentiate itself by focusing on AI-driven pre-screening and interview automation, adding Central European representation to a landscape otherwise dominated by Western and Southern European players.
“What impressed us most about Talentiqa were the strong Founders with a deep understanding of the problem. The startup leverages AI very effectively to tackle recruitment in industries where traditional approaches fail. They already have the trust of major corporations, which is a strong signal that their product works and has international potential,” says Jan Staněk, Managing Partner of Purple Ventures.
Founded in 2021, Talentiqa promises to make first-round interviews both faster and fairer, with its platform allegedly saving recruiters up to 100 hours per month and filling roles ten times quicker.
The startup’s founding duo consists of Marek Dian, who brings substantial business management experience, and Miro Šmelko, a seasoned software developer.
The startup’s technology is currently used by major employers including ČEZ, Raiffeisenbank, Bageterie Boulevard, and Manpower. By automating initial candidate screening with an AI assistant that operates around the clock, Talentiqa cuts out repetitive manual work and dramatically improves the speed at which employers can identify promising candidates.
While initial interviews traditionally rely on availability during working hours, Talentiqa says they flip the script, allowing applicants to chat with AI during evenings or weekends – times when job seekers are often more available.
The investment is set to accelerate Talentiqa’s rollout across Europe and beyond. The company was initially launched under the name Jobsider, in close collaboration with HR professionals to test features. It has since focused on its intelligent pre-screening core, rebranding to Talentiqa.
“It’s common that job seekers are still employed elsewhere. When recruiters call them during regular business hours, they’re often unavailable, don’t answer, and risk being dropped from the process. Our system allows applicants to complete the pre-screening through a chat with the AI assistant whenever it suits them – even at night or on weekends – which is key to our excellent results,” adds Marek Dian.
Talentiqa’s AI system is made up of dozens of interlinked agents that perform initial screening interviews, parse CVs (or even handle applications without them), and verify key information such as location, availability, and employment preferences. Candidates who pass are instantly advanced to the next round, while those who don’t still get entered into the company’s talent pool for future consideration.
The solution is particularly impactful for sectors with large recruitment volumes such as retail, logistics, food services, and customer support, but it’s also reportedly gaining traction in banking, telecommunications, and energy.
“We are constantly hiring for dozens of our restaurants. The most demanding part was always the initial round of calls – recruiters often spent half of their working hours just trying to reach candidates, as there are so many and few pick up the phone on the first try.
“Thanks to Talentiqa and its unique solution, we’ve been able to significantly speed up and streamline this process. This gives us more time to personally engage with applicants where it makes the most sense,” says Kristýna Krouželová, HR Manager at Bageterie Boulevard , which started using Talentiqa last year.
According to data provided by the company, Talentiqa boasts impressive results: up to 80% of recruiter time saved, 90% of candidates completing pre-screening within 24 hours, and more than 400% ROI for companies using the platform.
Notably, 93% of applicants reported they would prefer chat interviews as a standard part of the hiring process, and 88% felt more confident and less stressed than during traditional phone interviews.
With a strong product-market fit and a growing list of corporate clients, Talentiqa is positioning itself not just as a Czech recruitment innovation, but as a global contender in the HRTech space.
David Cendon Garcia

evidence.raw_content:
Logo
Logo
Logo

Logo
Madrid-based Anyformat raises €520k to simplify the automation of unstructured data extraction
Stefano De Marzo

Anyformat, a Spanish startup betting on a new era of collaboration between people and artificial intelligence, has announced the raising of €520k in a pre-seed financing round. This round, led by 4Founders Capital, Abac Nest Ventures, and prominent business angels, will allow anyformat to accelerate the development of its generative artificial intelligence platform, designed to transform the way companies manage and analyze unstructured data.
With this injection of capital, anyformat is consolidating itself as a key solution to automate the extraction and analysis of complex data, enabling any user – without the need for technical knowledge – to convert large volumes of unstructured information into strategic insights. This marks a crucial step for organizations looking to optimize processes and improve data-driven decision making.
According to the Data Cube report “Spain Artificial Intelligence Market Growth and Performance”, the artificial intelligence market in Spain is expected to reach $26.56 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 27.4%. This growth environment, combined with the exponential increase in the generation of unstructured data, reinforces the strategic relevance of anyformat for companies seeking operational agility and efficiency.
Juan Huguet, CEO of anyformat, said: “Our mission is clear: to enable organizations to adopt hybrid intelligence by allowing AI to do the heavy lifting of extracting and structuring data, and getting people to focus on solving strategic and creative problems. With anyformat, companies access valuable information quickly and without complications.”
The anyformat platform automates the extraction of information contained in documents, presentations or even voice recordings, simplifying what previously required a lot of manual work or advanced technical knowledge. In the words of Alejandro Fernández, COO of anyformat, “Many companies are held back by the difficulty of managing unstructured data. With our solution, we make that information immediately available to teams, ready to be integrated into tools such as Excel or business intelligence platforms.”
In addition to its focus on usability, anyformat is designed to scale easily, ensuring simple integration with existing systems. Diego Pérez, CTO of anyformat, added: “Our priority is to optimize workflows without compromising data security or privacy. We know that trust is key, and we ensure that information is handled responsibly and efficiently, allowing organizations to grow sustainably.”
Investors recognize anyformat’s disruptive potential. Joan Pina, partner at Abac Nest Ventures, commented: “At Abac Nest Ventures we have bet on anyformat because we believe in the vision of its founders and in the impact that its technology can have on the industry. Turning unstructured data into actionable knowledge is a growing need, and companies using anyformat are gaining competitiveness by quickly and efficiently integrating AI into their processes.”
With the new funding, anyformat is preparing to expand its team and operations, with an eye toward consolidating its presence in Spain and exploring opportunities in international markets. “At anyformat, we are committed to transforming unstructured information into a competitive advantage, allowing companies to not only access valuable information, but to use it intelligently to make strategic decisions that drive their growth and success,” concluded Juan Huguet.
Stefano De Marzo

By  Dylan Bailey    Updated   Category News   Read 4 min
Anyformat Secures €3.3 Million to Revolutionize Document Management
Funding Round Overview
Recently, Anyformat has successfully closed a €3.3 million seed funding round, with Kibo Ventures leading the investment. This round also saw participation from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which have previously supported the company. This capital will be instrumental in enhancing its innovative document management solutions.
Transforming Document Management
Anyformat specializes in offering generative AI solutions that simplify how organizations handle their documents. Their platform is designed to extract and structure complex data seamlessly, turning traditional document management approaches on their heads. This technology significantly speeds up the processing of various documents such as contracts, invoices, and presentations, achieving human-level accuracy while retaining strict control over sensitive data.
Broader Market Applications
Demonstrating its prowess, Anyformat has already collaborated with notable clients including the Singapore Government, L'Oréal Group, and IAG Group. These projects underline Its capacity to function effectively even in challenging and high-demand situations. The company has moved beyond mere document extraction, offering capabilities that merge extracted information with a company's proprietary data instantly.
Real-Time Analysis and Benefits
This unique offer allows for the automated analysis of unstructured documents while cross-referencing vital data with internal resources, such as customer databases and historical records. This technological advancement is particularly advantageous for organizations grappling with complex documentation and disjointed systems, largely mitigating manual validation steps and reducing the probability of errors.
Addressing Industry Needs
A significant challenge identified in a report by Dell Technologies indicates that a remarkable 68% of Spanish companies struggle to leverage their data for real-time insights. Anyformat is determined to bridge this gap, transforming document management into an asset for businesses rather than a burden.
Insights from Industry Leaders
Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner at Kibo Ventures, emphasizes the unique position of Anyformat within the document intelligence market, now shifting towards generative AI. He notes that the company’s cutting-edge technology empowers organizations to automate critical processes, a necessity for ensuring data sovereignty, especially within Europe.
Future Growth Projections
The company anticipates exceeding €1 million in annual recurring revenue by 2026, fueled by its anticipated expansion into Europe and the surging demand for advanced document processing solutions. Juan Huguet, the CEO and co-founder, stresses the importance of documents as the backbone of organizations, aiming to help businesses analyze them with both human intelligence and machine efficiency while adhering to modern security regulations.
Investment in Technology
The fresh funding will significantly boost product development and allow Anyformat to enhance its engineering and product teams, contributing to the advancement of its extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error-detection models. Moreover, a dedicated sales team focused on Spain and the European Union will be established to drive growth.
Commitment to Privacy Standards
Anyformat remains focused on maintaining a privacy-first infrastructure and aims to achieve relevant certifications such as ISO 27001, which is crucial for serving large organizations and public institutions. This commitment to data protection not only further solidifies its market position but also reassures clients about the handling of their sensitive information.
Frequently Asked Questions
What is Anyformat?
Anyformat is a generative AI platform that specializes in extracting and structuring complex data from various types of documents.
Who led the recent funding round for Anyformat?
Kibo Ventures led the €3.3 million seed funding round for Anyformat.
What are the key benefits of Anyformat's technology?
The technology allows organizations to process documents with high accuracy and speed, enabling better management of sensitive data.
How does Anyformat ensure data privacy?
Anyformat upholds rigorous privacy standards and works toward achieving certifications like ISO 27001 for enhanced data protection.
What growth does Anyformat anticipate?
Anyformat projects to surpass €1 million in annual recurring revenue in 2026 as it expands throughout Europe, driven by increasing demand for automation.

Anyformat closes a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures
anyformat_team
01/12/2025  |  sradmin
Anyformat,  a generative AI platform specialized in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures, with follow-on participation from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which also supported the company’s previous round.
The company is building the next generation of document infrastructure powered by generative AI. Its platform enables operational teams in large organizations to process complex documents with human-level accuracy at machine speed, while retaining full control over sensitive data. The technology is designed for seamless deployment from day one, requiring minimal technical dependencies.
Since its previous €520,000 pre-seed round, Anyformat has expanded its capabilities well beyond traditional extraction. It is now the only provider that can combine extracted document information with internal, proprietary company data in real time. This means that contracts, invoices, or emails can be automatically analyzed while being cross-referenced with corporate databases such as customer records, policies, product catalogs, or historical data.
For organizations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors, and accelerates critical business processes. This represents a real problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies fail to convert their data into valuable information in real time, highlighting the urgency for solutions such as Anyformat.
“”The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that allows companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key issue in Europe. The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai, a company also invested in by Kibo Ventures, combined with the possibilities offered by AI in a huge market, were key factors in our decision to invest,” says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner of Kibo Ventures.,” says Javier Torremocha, partner at Kibo Ventures.
With a team of nine full-time employees, the startup continues to refine its technology to convert any document, presentation, or file into data ready for analysis, business intelligence, or internal operations, always with the strictest privacy controls.
Growth underway: €1 million ARR forecast for 2026
Anyformat aims to exceed €1 million in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion in Europe and the increase in use cases linked to automation, compliance, and advanced document processing.
Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat, says “Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to enable companies to analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the security required by regulations. We are building the new European document infrastructure.” Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO of the company, adds, “We are developing technology capable of going far beyond document digitization.”
The investment: technology, expansion, and compliance
This new funding will enable the company to accelerate its product development and strengthen areas critical to its growth. A significant portion of the funds will be used to expand the engineering and product team, driving its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error detection models. At the same time, Anyformat will build a highly focused sales team in Spain and the European Union to capitalize on the growing demand for intelligent data processing solutions.
In addition, the company will continue to invest in its privacy-first infrastructure, strengthening security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001, which is key to working with large corporations and public administrations.
With this round of funding, Anyformat is accelerating its path to becoming the European benchmark in document AI. The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organizations that cannot compromise on accuracy or data control. In a market moving towards more autonomous, private, and multimodal solutions, Anyformat is setting the course for how generative AI will be applied in document management over the next decade.

Logo

Czech it out: HRTech startup Talentiqa raises €1 million to let AI do the first job interview – even at midnight
David Cendon Garcia
Talentiqa
Talentiqa, a recruitment automation startup out of Prague, has secured €1 million in funding to expand its AI-powered hiring assistant.
The round was equally backed by Purple Ventures and Venture to Future Fund.
“For twenty years I dealt with hiring hundreds of people in retail, hospitality, and gastronomy, and we constantly ran into the same issue. Companies complained there weren’t enough candidates, while applicants complained that firms didn’t know how to communicate with them – often not replying at all. These two worlds were simply missing each other. Sometimes the problem was as trivial as an applicant not being able to answer the phone in time.
“We founded Talentiqa to speed up the initial interview and increase its success rate. Thanks to AI, we can now automate the most difficult part at scale – the first contact, verifying interest, and quickly connecting the right people with the right companies,” says Marek Dian, Co-founder and CEO of Talentiqa.
In 2025, European HRTech startups have continued to attract steady investor interest, with several funding rounds reflecting the sector’s shift toward AI-driven hiring and workforce management.
Danish startup Kiku secured €4 million to address high-volume recruitment, while London-based Metaview raised around €30 million to expand its AI recruiting platform. In Southern Europe, Orbio in Madrid closed €6.4 million to scale its AI-native HCM system, and Skillvue in Milan raised €5.5 million to support skills-based recruitment.
Against this backdrop, Talentiqa’s round represents a comparatively smaller raise but positions it within the same European trend of automating hiring workflows through AI.
While its peers are advancing skills platforms, HCM systems, or payroll solutions, Talentiqa seems to differentiate itself by focusing on AI-driven pre-screening and interview automation, adding Central European representation to a landscape otherwise dominated by Western and Southern European players.
“What impressed us most about Talentiqa were the strong Founders with a deep understanding of the problem. The startup leverages AI very effectively to tackle recruitment in industries where traditional approaches fail. They already have the trust of major corporations, which is a strong signal that their product works and has international potential,” says Jan Staněk, Managing Partner of Purple Ventures.
Founded in 2021, Talentiqa promises to make first-round interviews both faster and fairer, with its platform allegedly saving recruiters up to 100 hours per month and filling roles ten times quicker.
The startup’s founding duo consists of Marek Dian, who brings substantial business management experience, and Miro Šmelko, a seasoned software developer.
The startup’s technology is currently used by major employers including ČEZ, Raiffeisenbank, Bageterie Boulevard, and Manpower. By automating initial candidate screening with an AI assistant that operates around the clock, Talentiqa cuts out repetitive manual work and dramatically improves the speed at which employers can identify promising candidates.
While initial interviews traditionally rely on availability during working hours, Talentiqa says they flip the script, allowing applicants to chat with AI during evenings or weekends – times when job seekers are often more available.
The investment is set to accelerate Talentiqa’s rollout across Europe and beyond. The company was initially launched under the name Jobsider, in close collaboration with HR professionals to test features. It has since focused on its intelligent pre-screening core, rebranding to Talentiqa.
“It’s common that job seekers are still employed elsewhere. When recruiters call them during regular business hours, they’re often unavailable, don’t answer, and risk being dropped from the process. Our system allows applicants to complete the pre-screening through a chat with the AI assistant whenever it suits them – even at night or on weekends – which is key to our excellent results,” adds Marek Dian.
Talentiqa’s AI system is made up of dozens of interlinked agents that perform initial screening interviews, parse CVs (or even handle applications without them), and verify key information such as location, availability, and employment preferences. Candidates who pass are instantly advanced to the next round, while those who don’t still get entered into the company’s talent pool for future consideration.
The solution is particularly impactful for sectors with large recruitment volumes such as retail, logistics, food services, and customer support, but it’s also reportedly gaining traction in banking, telecommunications, and energy.
“We are constantly hiring for dozens of our restaurants. The most demanding part was always the initial round of calls – recruiters often spent half of their working hours just trying to reach candidates, as there are so many and few pick up the phone on the first try.
“Thanks to Talentiqa and its unique solution, we’ve been able to significantly speed up and streamline this process. This gives us more time to personally engage with applicants where it makes the most sense,” says Kristýna Krouželová, HR Manager at Bageterie Boulevard , which started using Talentiqa last year.
According to data provided by the company, Talentiqa boasts impressive results: up to 80% of recruiter time saved, 90% of candidates completing pre-screening within 24 hours, and more than 400% ROI for companies using the platform.
Notably, 93% of applicants reported they would prefer chat interviews as a standard part of the hiring process, and 88% felt more confident and less stressed than during traditional phone interviews.
With a strong product-market fit and a growing list of corporate clients, Talentiqa is positioning itself not just as a Czech recruitment innovation, but as a global contender in the HRTech space.
David Cendon Garcia

Anyformat closes a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures
anyformat_team
01/12/2025  |  sradmin
Anyformat,  a generative AI platform specialized in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures, with follow-on participation from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which also supported the company’s previous round.
The company is building the next generation of document infrastructure powered by generative AI. Its platform enables operational teams in large organizations to process complex documents with human-level accuracy at machine speed, while retaining full control over sensitive data. The technology is designed for seamless deployment from day one, requiring minimal technical dependencies.
Since its previous €520,000 pre-seed round, Anyformat has expanded its capabilities well beyond traditional extraction. It is now the only provider that can combine extracted document information with internal, proprietary company data in real time. This means that contracts, invoices, or emails can be automatically analyzed while being cross-referenced with corporate databases such as customer records, policies, product catalogs, or historical data.
For organizations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors, and accelerates critical business processes. This represents a real problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies fail to convert their data into valuable information in real time, highlighting the urgency for solutions such as Anyformat.
“”The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that allows companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key issue in Europe. The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai, a company also invested in by Kibo Ventures, combined with the possibilities offered by AI in a huge market, were key factors in our decision to invest,” says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner of Kibo Ventures.,” says Javier Torremocha, partner at Kibo Ventures.
With a team of nine full-time employees, the startup continues to refine its technology to convert any document, presentation, or file into data ready for analysis, business intelligence, or internal operations, always with the strictest privacy controls.
Growth underway: €1 million ARR forecast for 2026
Anyformat aims to exceed €1 million in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion in Europe and the increase in use cases linked to automation, compliance, and advanced document processing.
Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat, says “Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to enable companies to analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the security required by regulations. We are building the new European document infrastructure.” Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO of the company, adds, “We are developing technology capable of going far beyond document digitization.”
The investment: technology, expansion, and compliance
This new funding will enable the company to accelerate its product development and strengthen areas critical to its growth. A significant portion of the funds will be used to expand the engineering and product team, driving its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error detection models. At the same time, Anyformat will build a highly focused sales team in Spain and the European Union to capitalize on the growing demand for intelligent data processing solutions.
In addition, the company will continue to invest in its privacy-first infrastructure, strengthening security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001, which is key to working with large corporations and public administrations.
With this round of funding, Anyformat is accelerating its path to becoming the European benchmark in document AI. The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organizations that cannot compromise on accuracy or data control. In a market moving towards more autonomous, private, and multimodal solutions, Anyformat is setting the course for how generative AI will be applied in document management over the next decade.

Madrid-based Anyformat raises €520k to simplify the automation of unstructured data extraction
Stefano De Marzo

Anyformat, a Spanish startup betting on a new era of collaboration between people and artificial intelligence, has announced the raising of €520k in a pre-seed financing round. This round, led by 4Founders Capital, Abac Nest Ventures, and prominent business angels, will allow anyformat to accelerate the development of its generative artificial intelligence platform, designed to transform the way companies manage and analyze unstructured data.
With this injection of capital, anyformat is consolidating itself as a key solution to automate the extraction and analysis of complex data, enabling any user – without the need for technical knowledge – to convert large volumes of unstructured information into strategic insights. This marks a crucial step for organizations looking to optimize processes and improve data-driven decision making.
According to the Data Cube report “Spain Artificial Intelligence Market Growth and Performance”, the artificial intelligence market in Spain is expected to reach $26.56 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 27.4%. This growth environment, combined with the exponential increase in the generation of unstructured data, reinforces the strategic relevance of anyformat for companies seeking operational agility and efficiency.
Juan Huguet, CEO of anyformat, said: “Our mission is clear: to enable organizations to adopt hybrid intelligence by allowing AI to do the heavy lifting of extracting and structuring data, and getting people to focus on solving strategic and creative problems. With anyformat, companies access valuable information quickly and without complications.”
The anyformat platform automates the extraction of information contained in documents, presentations or even voice recordings, simplifying what previously required a lot of manual work or advanced technical knowledge. In the words of Alejandro Fernández, COO of anyformat, “Many companies are held back by the difficulty of managing unstructured data. With our solution, we make that information immediately available to teams, ready to be integrated into tools such as Excel or business intelligence platforms.”
In addition to its focus on usability, anyformat is designed to scale easily, ensuring simple integration with existing systems. Diego Pérez, CTO of anyformat, added: “Our priority is to optimize workflows without compromising data security or privacy. We know that trust is key, and we ensure that information is handled responsibly and efficiently, allowing organizations to grow sustainably.”
Investors recognize anyformat’s disruptive potential. Joan Pina, partner at Abac Nest Ventures, commented: “At Abac Nest Ventures we have bet on anyformat because we believe in the vision of its founders and in the impact that its technology can have on the industry. Turning unstructured data into actionable knowledge is a growing need, and companies using anyformat are gaining competitiveness by quickly and efficiently integrating AI into their processes.”
With the new funding, anyformat is preparing to expand its team and operations, with an eye toward consolidating its presence in Spain and exploring opportunities in international markets. “At anyformat, we are committed to transforming unstructured information into a competitive advantage, allowing companies to not only access valuable information, but to use it intelligently to make strategic decisions that drive their growth and success,” concluded Juan Huguet.
Stefano De Marzo
